To address the limited computational capacity of CSDs, we further propose a zero-skipping encoding strategy at the protocol level, which decouples actual payloads from paddings to avoid redundant computations. Additionally, SmartPIR incorporates two key architectural optimizations: (1) a resource-efficient FPGA circuit design and (2) a load-aware scheduling strategy, which collectively sustain high throughput and ensure scalability. Implemented on a commercial off-the-shelf CSD array, SmartPIR achieves a 10 2 × ∼ 10 3 × speedup over state-of-the-art CPU-based PIR schemes.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于全同态加密的隐私信息检索系统通过对不受信任的服务器托管的数据库启用加密查询来提供强大的隐私性。然而，采用受到系统级瓶颈的限制，包括大规模设置中的严格 I/O 限制以及处理可变长度数据的低效率。为了解决这些问题，我们推出了 SmartPIR，这是一种可扩展的 PIR 系统，可通过协议和架构协同设计来应对这些挑战。首先，我们引入了一个存储内计算框架，该框架将 FHE 操作卸载到计算存储设备 (CSD)，从而消除大量数据移动的开销。为了解决 CSD 有限的计算能力，我们进一步在协议级别提出了一种零跳跃编码策略，它将实际有效负载与填充解耦，以避免冗余计算。此外，SmartPIR 还整合了两项关键架构优化：(1) 资源高效型 FPGA 电路设计和 (2) 负载感知调度策略，共同维持高吞吐量并确保可扩展性。 SmartPIR 在商用现成 CSD 阵列上实施，与最先进的基于 CPU 的 PIR 方案相比，速度提高了 10 2 × ∼ 10 3 ×。

papers.title: SMX: Heterogeneous Architecture for Universal Sequence Alignment Acceleration.
papers.abstract: Sequence alignment is a fundamental building block for critical applications across multiple fields, such as computational biology and information retrieval. The rapid advancement of genome sequencing technologies and breakthrough generative AI tools, like AlphaFold, has driven an exponential increase in sequence-data production, creating a pressing need for fast and efficient sequence alignment tools to analyze ever-growing biological sequence databases. Notwithstanding the numerous accelerators proposed, from general-purpose architectures (CPUs and GPUs) to domain-specific designs (FPGAs and ASICs), the most efficient solutions suffer from over-specialization and fail to adapt to the wide variety of irregular use cases demanded by practical sequence alignment applications. Thus, it remains a challenge to design an architecture that can balance efficiency and flexibility to meet the demands of real-world alignment applications. This work introduces SMX, a heterogeneous architecture designed for high-performance sequence alignment that supports various configurations for different sequence types (DNA, protein, and ASCII text) and alignment models (including weighted gaps and substitution matrices). SMX integrates an ISA extension (SMX-1D) for irregular and sequential tasks and a specialized coprocessor (SMX-2D) to accelerate regular and parallel tasks, both orchestrated by the general-purpose core to enable seamless integration with state-of-the-art sequence alignment algorithms. Our results demonstrate that SMX’s heterogeneous architecture accelerates different sequence alignment use cases by 256–744 × compared to state-of-the-art software implementations when aligning real datasets. Compared to specialized hardware accelerators, SMX delivers up to 18.5 × more peak performance per area added while providing greater flexibility to accelerate different use cases. Physical design results targeting a 22nm technology node estimate SMX’s area at 0.34mm 2 , which is only 30% of a single-issue in-order CPU. SMX offers a high-performance and efficient heterogeneous architecture for accelerating practical sequence alignment algorithms, providing a scalable and flexible solution tailored to meet the needs of modern sequence-analysis tools. Furthermore, an SMX case study explores the frontier between flexibility and efficiency in domain-specific architectures and accelerators.
papers.abstract_zh: 序列比对是计算生物学和信息检索等多个领域关键应用的基本构建模块。基因组测序技术的快速发展和突破性的生成人工智能工具（如 AlphaFold）推动了序列数据产量的指数级增长，迫切需要快速高效的序列比对工具来分析不断增长的生物序列数据库。尽管提出了众多加速器，从通用架构（CPU 和 GPU）到特定领域设计（FPGA 和 ASIC），但最高效的解决方案都存在过度专业化的问题，并且无法适应实际序列比对应用所需的各种不规则用例。因此，设计一种能够平衡效率和灵活性以满足实际对准应用需求的架构仍然是一个挑战。这项工作引入了 SMX，这是一种专为高性能序列比对而设计的异构架构，支持不同序列类型（DNA、蛋白质和 ASCII 文本）和比对模型（包括加权间隙和替换矩阵）的各种配置。 SMX 集成了用于不规则和顺序任务的 ISA 扩展 (SMX-1D) 以及用于加速常规和并行任务的专用协处理器 (SMX-2D)，两者均由通用内核精心编排，以实现与最先进的序列比对算法的无缝集成。我们的结果表明，在对齐真实数据集时，与最先进的软件实现相比，SMX 的异构架构将不同的序列对齐用例加速了 256-744 倍。与专用硬件加速器相比，SMX 每增加一个面积即可提供高达 18.5 倍的峰值性能，同时提供更大的灵活性来加速不同的用例。针对 22nm 技术节点的物理设计结果估计 SMX 的面积为 0.34mm 2 ，仅为单发有序 CPU 的 30%。 SMX 提供高性能、高效的异构架构，用于加速实用的序列比对算法，提供可扩展且灵活的解决方案，以满足现代序列分析工具的需求。此外，SMX 案例研究探索了特定领域架构和加速器的灵活性和效率之间的前沿。

papers.title: SoftWalker: Supporting Software Page Table Walk for Irregular GPU Applications.
papers.abstract:
Address translation has become a significant and growing performance bottleneck in modern GPUs, especially for emerging irregular applications with high TLB miss rates. The limited concurrency of hardware Page Table Walkers (PTWs), due to their small and fixed number, causes severe contention and substantial queueing delays under high translation pressure, which significantly degrades performance.

This paper introduces SoftWalker , a novel, scalable, and flexible framework that fundamentally shifts the GPU page table walking from fixed-function hardware to software execution. SoftWalker leverages the GPU’s massive thread-level parallelism by dynamically dispatching specialized, lightweight software threads running on GPU cores to handle TLB misses requiring page table walks. In addition, to expand L2 TLB MSHR capacity on demand, SoftWalker incorporates In-TLB MSHRs, a key innovation that repurposes underutilized L2 TLB entries to track outstanding misses when existing MSHRs are saturated. By alleviating MSHR-induced contention, this design preserves the key advantage of highly parallel page table walking in software. SoftWalker enables thousands of concurrent page table walks, significantly reducing PTW-level contention and translation queueing delays. As a result, it achieves an average reduction of 72.8% in page walk latency and delivers an average speedup of 2.24 × (3.94 × for irregular workloads).
papers.abstract_zh:
地址转换已成为现代 GPU 中一个重要且不断增长的性能瓶颈，特别是对于 TLB 缺失率较高的新兴不规则应用程序。硬件页表遍历器（PTW）由于其数量小且固定，并发性有限，在高翻译压力下会导致严重的争用和大量的排队延迟，从而显着降低性能。

本文介绍了 SoftWalker，这是一种新颖、可扩展且灵活的框架，它从根本上将 GPU 页表遍历从固定功能硬件转变为软件执行。 SoftWalker 通过动态分派在 GPU 内核上运行的专用轻量级软件线程来处理需要页表遍历的 TLB 未命中，从而利用 GPU 的大规模线程级并行性。此外，为了按需扩展 L2 TLB MSHR 容量，SoftWalker 还采用了 In-TLB MSHR，这是一项关键创新，可重新调整未充分利用的 L2 TLB 条目的用途，以在现有 MSHR 饱和时跟踪突出的未命中情况。通过减轻 MSHR 引起的争用，该设计保留了软件中高度并行页表遍历的关键优势。 SoftWalker 支持数千个并发页表遍历，显着减少 PTW 级别的争用和转换排队延迟。结果，它的页面遍历延迟平均减少了 72.8%，平均加速率为 2.24 倍（不规则工作负载为 3.94 倍）。

papers.title: Software Prefetch Multicast: Sharer-Exposed Prefetching for Bandwidth Efficiency in Manycore Processors.
papers.abstract: As the core counts continue to scale in manycore processors, the increasing bandwidth pressure on the network-on-chip (NoC) and last-level cache (LLC) emerges as a critical performance bottleneck. While shared-data multicasting from the LLC can alleviate this pressure by combining responses heading to the same direction, existing solutions face fundamental challenges in accurately identifying complete sharer sets and avoiding unnecessary data responses. In this work, we propose Software Prefetch Multicast (SPM) , a software-hardware co-design that addresses these limitations through sharer-exposed prefetching for bandwidth-efficient multicast. SPM introduces three key innovations: (1) New software-hardware interfaces including sharer group configuration and sharer-exposed prefetching instructions, enabling software-directed multicast initiation; (2) A corresponding microarchitecture for sharer group configuration that supports sharer-group-based multicast triggering in LLC through representative prefetch requests from leader threads; and (3) A dynamic leader thread switching algorithm to accommodate thread variation. Compared with a system with software prefetching, SPM achieves an average of 42% NoC bandwidth saving, a geometric mean of 1.28 × and a maximum of 1.46 × speedup in a 16-core system. In a 64-core system, it can achieve an average of 50% NoC bandwidth saving, along with a geomean of 1.38 × and a maximum of 1.86 × speedup.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着多核处理器中的核心数量不断增加，片上网络 (NoC) 和末级缓存 (LLC) 上不断增加的带宽压力成为关键的性能瓶颈。虽然来自 LLC 的共享数据多播可以通过组合前往同一方向的响应来减轻这种压力，但现有解决方案在准确识别完整共享者集和避免不必要的数据响应方面面临着根本挑战。在这项工作中，我们提出了软件预取多播（SPM），这是一种软件硬件协同设计，通过共享者公开的预取来实现带宽高效的多播来解决这些限制。 SPM引入了三个关键创新：（1）新的软件-硬件接口，包括共享器组配置和共享器公开的预取指令，从而实现软件引导的多播启动； (2)用于共享器组配置的相应微体系结构，通过来自领导线程的代表性预取请求来支持LLC中基于共享器组的多播触发； (3)动态引导线程切换算法以适应线程变化。与采用软件预取的系统相比，SPM在16核系统中平均节省42%的NoC带宽，几何平均1.28倍，最大加速1.46倍。在64核系统中，它可以实现平均50%的NoC带宽节省，以及1.38×的几何平均值和最大1.86×的加速比。

papers.title: Sonar: A Hardware Fuzzing Framework to Uncover Contention Side Channels in Processors.
papers.abstract:
Contention-based side channels, rooted in resource sharing, have emerged as a significant security threat in modern processors. These side channels allow attackers to leverage timing differences caused by conflicts in execution ports, caches, or interconnects to infer secret information such as cryptographic keys or enclave-resident data. Despite increasing awareness, detecting such channels remains challenging because triggering contentions requires precisely orchestrating specific microarchitectural states, which is often difficult in practice, especially for timing-sensitive contentions.

This paper introduces Sonar , the first systematic and automated fuzzing framework designed to uncover contention side channels in processors. Our core idea is to leverage microarchitectural states to guide testcase generation, enabling the precise triggering of microarchitectural events with stringent conditions. Sonar is built on the key observation that multiplexers (MUXes) are hotspots for contention, as resource contention frequently involves data routing and signal selection, which are fundamentally implemented by MUXes in circuits. We first identify contention-critical states with side channel risks based on MUXes, and then utilize these runtime states to directly guide testcase generation via fuzzing, progressively approaching and ultimately triggering contentions. Finally, we employ a dual-differential comparison method to efficiently detect contention-induced side channels and simulate attack scenarios to assess their exploitability.

Evaluated on two out-of-order RISC-V processors, Sonar uncovers 14 contention side channels, including 11 previously unknown. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of Sonar in uncovering potentially exploitable microarchitectural contentions.
papers.abstract_zh:
植根于资源共享的基于争用的侧通道已成为现代处理器中的重大安全威胁。这些侧通道允许攻击者利用执行端口、缓存或互连冲突引起的时序差异来推断秘密信息，例如加密密钥或飞地驻留数据。尽管意识不断增强，检测此类通道仍然具有挑战性，因为触发争用需要精确编排特定的微架构状态，这在实践中通常很困难，特别是对于时间敏感的争用。

本文介绍了 Sonar，这是第一个系统化、自动化的模糊测试框架，旨在发现处理器中的争用侧通道。我们的核心思想是利用微架构状态来指导测试用例生成，从而能够在严格的条件下精确触发微架构事件。 Sonar 建立在以下关键观察之上：多路复用器 (MUX) 是争用热点，因为资源争用经常涉及数据路由和信号选择，而这些基本上是由电路中的 MUX 实现的。我们首先基于 MUX 识别具有侧通道风险的争用关键状态，然后利用这些运行时状态通过模糊测试直接指导测试用例生成，逐步接近并最终触发争用。最后，我们采用双差分比较方法来有效检测争用引起的侧信道并模拟攻击场景以评估其可利用性。

在两个无序 RISC-V 处理器上进行评估后，Sonar 发现了 14 个争用侧通道，其中包括 11 个以前未知的通道。这些结果证明了声纳在发现潜在可利用的微架构争用方面的有效性。

papers.title: Stratum: System-Hardware Co-Design with Tiered Monolithic 3D-Stackable DRAM for Efficient MoE Serving.
papers.abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) continue to evolve, Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture has emerged as a prevailing design for achieving state-of-the-art performance across a wide range of tasks. MoE models use sparse gating to activate only a handful of expert sub-networks per input, achieving billion-parameter capacity with inference costs akin to much smaller models. However, such models often pose challenges for hardware deployment due to the massive data volume introduced by the MoE layers. To address the challenges of serving MoE models, we propose Stratum, a system–hardware co-design approach that combines the novel memory technology Monolithic 3D-Stackable DRAM (Mono3D DRAM), near-memory processing (NMP), and GPU acceleration. The logic and Mono3D DRAM dies are connected through hybrid bonding, whereas the Mono3D DRAM stack and GPU are interconnected via silicon interposer. Mono3D DRAM offers higher internal bandwidth than HBM thanks to the dense vertical interconnect pitch enabled by its monolithic structure, which supports implementations of higher-performance near-memory processing. Furthermore, we tackle the latency differences introduced by aggressive vertical scaling of Mono3D DRAM along the z -dimension by constructing internal memory tiers and assigning data across layers based on access likelihood, guided by topic-based expert usage prediction to boost NMP throughput. The Stratum system achieves up to 8.29 × improvement in decoding throughput and 7.66 × better energy efficiency across various benchmarks compared to GPU baselines.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型 (LLM) 的不断发展，专家混合 (MoE) 架构已成为在各种任务中实现最先进性能的流行设计。 MoE 模型使用稀疏门控每个输入仅激活少数专家子网络，从而实现十亿参数容量，推理成本类似于小得多的模型。然而，由于 MoE 层引入的海量数据量，此类模型通常会给硬件部署带来挑战。为了解决服务 MoE 模型的挑战，我们提出了 Stratum，这是一种系统硬件协同设计方法，结合了新颖的内存技术单片 3D 可堆叠 DRAM (Mono3D DRAM)、近内存处理 (NMP) 和 GPU 加速。逻辑和 Mono3D DRAM 芯片通过混合键合连接，而 Mono3D DRAM 堆栈和 GPU 通过硅中介层互连。由于其单片结构实现了密集的垂直互连间距，Mono3D DRAM 提供比 HBM 更高的内部带宽，支持实现更高性能的近内存处理。此外，我们通过构建内部存储器层并根据访问可能性跨层分配数据，在基于主题的专家使用预测的指导下提高 NMP 吞吐量，解决了 Mono3D DRAM 沿 z 维度的积极垂直扩展所带来的延迟差异。与 GPU 基准相比，Stratum 系统在各种基准测试中的解码吞吐量提高了 8.29 倍，能源效率提高了 7.66 倍。

papers.title: StreamTensor: Make Tensors Stream in Dataflow Accelerators for LLMs.
papers.abstract: Efficient execution of deep learning workloads on dataflow architectures is crucial for overcoming memory bottlenecks and maximizing performance. While streaming intermediate results between computation kernels can significantly improve efficiency, existing approaches struggle with inter-kernel correlations, external memory access management, and buffer optimization. In this work, we propose StreamTensor, a compiler framework that automatically constructs and optimizes stream-based dataflow accelerators. StreamTensor introduces a novel iterative tensor type system to explicitly encode stream layouts, enabling seamless kernel fusion, buffer allocation, and memory optimization. By systematically exploring three hierarchical design spaces, including tensor tiling, kernel fusion, and resource allocation, StreamTensor balances computational intensity, memory efficiency, and data streaming to maximize performance. Based on FPGA evaluations on Large Language Models (LLM), StreamTensor achieves up to 0.76x and 0.64x lower latency compared to the state-of-the-art FPGA LLM accelerators and GPUs, and up to 1.99x higher energy efficiency compared to GPUs, making it a promising approach for scalable dataflow-based deep learning acceleration.
papers.abstract_zh: 在数据流架构上高效执行深度学习工作负载对于克服内存瓶颈和最大限度地提高性能至关重要。虽然在计算内核之间流式传输中间结果可以显着提高效率，但现有方法在内核间关联、外部内存访问管理和缓冲区优化方面遇到了困难。在这项工作中，我们提出了 StreamTensor，这是一个编译器框架，可以自动构建和优化基于流的数据流加速器。 StreamTensor 引入了一种新颖的迭代张量类型系统来显式编码流布局，从而实现无缝内核融合、缓冲区分配和内存优化。通过系统地探索三个层次设计空间，包括张量平铺、内核融合和资源分配，StreamTensor 平衡了计算强度、内存效率和数据流，以最大限度地提高性能。基于大型语言模型 (LLM) 上的 FPGA 评估，与最先进的 FPGA LLM 加速器和 GPU 相比，StreamTensor 的延迟分别降低了 0.76 倍和 0.64 倍，与 GPU 相比，能源效率提高了 1.99 倍，这使其成为基于可扩展数据流的深度学习加速的一种有前途的方法。

papers.title: SuperMesh: Energy-Efficient Collective Communications for Accelerators.
papers.abstract: Chiplet-based Deep Neural Network (DNN) accelerators are a promising approach to meet the scalability demands of modern DNN models. Such accelerators usually utilize 2D mesh topologies. However, state-of-the-art collective communication algorithms often struggle within these topologies due to limited connectivity at border nodes, leading to communication bottlenecks and performance degradation. To address this challenge, we propose two novel topologies for chiplet-based accelerators aimed at improving collective communication performance and energy efficiency by integrating additional links parallel to the existing peripheral links of mesh topologies. The first proposed topology, SuperMesh Bi adds bidirectional links parallel to all peripheral links. In contrast, the second proposed topology, SuperMesh Alter , alternately adds bidirectional links parallel to the peripheral links, offering additional paths for data traversal. Both of the topologies adhere to a core principle—augmenting the outer region of mesh topologies with extra links to retain the original structure’s latency and scalability, ensuring compatibility with chiplet-based accelerator designs and maintaining energy efficiency. To fully utilize these enhanced topologies, we co-designed pipelined collective algorithms for AllReduce, ReduceScatter, and AllGather. Our proposed algorithms and topologies achieve an average AllReduce speedup of 1.18 − 1.33 × and a 1.77 − 2.22 × speedup in ReduceScatter and AllGather compared to conventional 2D-mesh topologies.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于 Chiplet 的深度神经网络 (DNN) 加速器是满足现代 DNN 模型的可扩展性需求的一种有前途的方法。此类加速器通常采用 2D 网格拓扑。然而，由于边界节点的连接有限，最先进的集体通信算法经常在这些拓扑中陷入困境，导致通信瓶颈和性能下降。为了应对这一挑战，我们为基于小芯片的加速器提出了两种新颖的拓扑，旨在通过集成与网状拓扑现有外围链路并行的附加链路来提高集体通信性能和能源效率。 SuperMesh Bi 第一个提出的拓扑添加了与所有外围链路并行的双向链路。相比之下，第二种提出的拓扑 SuperMesh Alter 交替添加与外围链路并行的双向链路，为数据遍历提供额外的路径。这两种拓扑都遵循一个核心原则——用额外的链路增强网状拓扑的外部区域，以保留原始结构的延迟和可扩展性，确保与基于小芯片的加速器设计的兼容性并保持能源效率。为了充分利用这些增强的拓扑，我们共同设计了 AllReduce、ReduceScatter 和 AllGather 的流水线集体算法。与传统的 2D 网格拓扑相比，我们提出的算法和拓扑实现了 1.18 − 1.33 × 的平均 AllReduce 加速，以及 ReduceScatter 和 AllGather 的 1.77 − 2.22 × 加速。

papers.title: SuperSFQ: A Hardware Design to Realize High-Frequency Superconducting Processors.
papers.abstract:
Superconducting computing using single flux quantum (SFQ) technology has been recognized as a promising post-Moore’s law era technology thanks to its extremely low power and high performance. Therefore, many researchers have proposed various SFQ-based circuits (e.g., ALU, register file) and architectures (e.g., NPU, CPU) to exploit the potential. However, due to the absence of a reliable and high-frequency clocking scheme, general SFQ circuits cannot operate at high frequencies, making all architectural efforts for high-performance SFQ computing ineffective.

In this paper, we propose SuperSFQ, a new design methodology for SFQ hardware that unlocks the high-frequency potential of SFQ technology by co-designing the clocking scheme, circuitry, and architecture. First, we propose SuperClocking, a new clocking scheme that enables high frequency in general SFQ hardware. Second, we implement an SFQ-based synchronizer to realize the reliable operation of SuperClocking. Finally, we provide two architectural design guidelines and corresponding solutions to ensure the functional correctness of SuperClocking in general SFQ devices.

By applying our clocking scheme, synchronizer, and guidelines to the latest general-purpose SFQ CPU, SuperSFQ achieves up to 62.5 times higher frequency and improves single-thread and multi-thread performance by 17 and 62.5 times, respectively, compared to conventional designs, with only 34.4% Josephson junction overhead. In addition, to demonstrate the generality of SuperSFQ, we apply SuperSFQ to 48 different benchmark circuits, achieving 88.5 times higher frequency compared to conventional designs, on average.
papers.abstract_zh:
使用单通量量子（SFQ）技术的超导计算因其极低的功耗和高性能而被认为是一种有前途的后摩尔定律时代技术。因此，许多研究人员提出了各种基于 SFQ 的电路（例如 ALU、寄存器文件）和架构（例如 NPU、CPU）来开发潜力。然而，由于缺乏可靠的高频时钟方案，通用SFQ电路无法在高频下运行，使得高性能SFQ计算的所有架构努力都无效。

在本文中，我们提出了 SuperSFQ，这是一种 SFQ 硬件的新设计方法，通过共同设计时钟方案、电路和架构来释放 SFQ 技术的高频潜力。首先，我们提出了 SuperClocking，这是一种新的时钟方案，可在通用 SFQ 硬件中实现高频。其次，我们实现了一个基于SFQ的同步器来实现SuperClocking的可靠运行。最后，我们提供了两个架构设计指南和相应的解决方案，以确保 SuperClocking 在通用 SFQ 器件中的功能正确性。

通过将我们的时钟方案、同步器和指南应用于最新的通用 SFQ CPU，与传统设计相比，SuperSFQ 的频率提高了 62.5 倍，单线程和多线程性能分别提高了 17 倍和 62.5 倍，而约瑟夫森结开销仅为 34.4%。此外，为了证明SuperSFQ的通用性，我们将SuperSFQ应用于48个不同的基准电路，与传统设计相比，平均实现了88.5倍的频率。

papers.title: Swift and Trustworthy Large-Scale GPU Simulation with Fine-Grained Error Modeling and Hierarchical Clustering.
papers.abstract:
Kernel-level sampling is an effective technique for running large-scale GPU workloads on cycle-level simulators by selecting a representative subset of kernels, thereby significantly reducing simulation complexity and runtime. However, in large-scale GPU workloads, kernels often exhibit heterogeneous runtime behaviors where some identical kernels show fluctuating performance, while others display multiple performance saturation points. We observe that the kernel execution time distribution is a powerful signature for addressing this complexity. By carefully analyzing execution time distributions, we show that heterogeneous kernels can be effectively classified and sampled, significantly reducing errors in sampled simulations.

This paper proposes STEM+ROOT, a fine-grained kernel-level sampling methodology that enables trustworthy sampled simulation by achieving minimal sampling error. STEM leverages the distribution of kernel execution times as a signature and applies statistical techniques to determine optimal sample sizes with tight error bounds. ROOT is a novel hierarchical clustering framework built on top of STEM that ensures the sampled kernels faithfully represent the entire workload in terms of execution time and a wide range of microarchitectural metrics. STEM achieves high scalability for large-scale GPU workloads by significantly reducing offline profiling overhead for collecting kernel execution times. When evaluated on the latest GPU benchmark suite, our proposed methodology reduces sampling error by 27.6-81.9 × and achieves 7-600 × faster kernel profiling than existing approaches while achieving comparable performance.
papers.abstract_zh:
内核级采样是一种有效的技术，通过选择内核的代表性子集，在周期级模拟器上运行大规模 GPU 工作负载，从而显着降低模拟复杂性和运行时间。然而，在大规模 GPU 工作负载中，内核通常会表现出异构运行时行为，其中一些相同的内核表现出波动的性能，而其他内核则表现出多个性能饱和点。我们观察到，内核执行时间分布是解决这种复杂性的有力标志。通过仔细分析执行时间分布，我们表明可以有效地对异构内核进行分类和采样，从而显着减少采样模拟中的错误。

本文提出了 STEM+ROOT，这是一种细粒度内核级采样方法，可通过实现最小采样误差来实现可信的采样模拟。 STEM 利用内核执行时间的分布作为签名，并应用统计技术来确定具有严格误差范围的最佳样本大小。 ROOT 是一种构建在 STEM 之上的新型分层集群框架，可确保采样的内核在执行时间和各种微体系结构指标方面忠实地代表整个工作负载。 STEM 通过显着减少收集内核执行时间的离线分析开销，实现大规模 GPU 工作负载的高可扩展性。在最新的 GPU 基准测试套件上进行评估时，我们提出的方法将采样误差降低了 27.6-81.9 倍，并且内核分析速度比现有方法快 7-600 倍，同时实现了相当的性能。

papers.title: SymbFuzz: Symbolic Execution Guided Hardware Fuzzing.
papers.abstract: Modern hardware incorporates reusable designs to reduce cost and time to market, inadvertently increasing exposure to security vulnerabilities. While formal verification and simulation-based approaches have been traditionally utilized to mitigate these vulnerabilities, formal techniques are hindered by scalability issues, while conventional simulation methods frequently overlook critical edge cases. Fuzzing, as a simulation-based strategy, has demonstrated considerable promise in enhancing the security of both software and hardware; however, it is impeded by challenges such as limited input coverage, difficulties in traversing branching paths, and the complexity of managing circuit parameters, in addition to the limited adaptability of existing hardware fuzzing techniques within industrial workflows. To address these limitations, we propose SymbFuzz , an innovative hybrid hardware fuzzing methodology that leverages symbolic execution to achieve superior coverage. SymbFuzz is the first hardware fuzzing technique to be implemented on the industry-standard Universal Verification Methodology (UVM), facilitating seamless integration into commercial hardware verification flows. SymbFuzz was evaluated on a diverse set of processor RTLs, including OpenTitan (Ibex), CVA6, Rocket-Core, and Mor1kx. These designs span a range of processor architectures and complexities. SymbFuzz detected all bugs previously found by existing fuzzers and additionally uncovered 14 new bugs, including a vulnerability in OpenTitan, reported in the CWE 2025 database. It also achieved up to 6.8 × faster convergence compared to traditional UVM random testing and over 2 × 10 4 additional functional coverage points compared to state-of-the-art fuzzers, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving RTL validation across varied processor architectures.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代硬件采用可重复使用的设计来降低成本和缩短上市时间，但无意中增加了安全漏洞的风险。虽然传统上使用形式验证和基于模拟的方法来缓解这些漏洞，但形式化技术受到可扩展性问题的阻碍，而传统的模拟方法经常忽视关键的边缘情况。模糊测试作为一种基于模拟的策略，在增强软件和硬件的安全性方面表现出了巨大的前景；然而，除了工业工作流程中现有硬件模糊测试技术的适应性有限之外，它还受到诸如有限的输入覆盖范围、遍历分支路径的困难以及管理电路参数的复杂性等挑战的阻碍。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 SymbFuzz，这是一种创新的混合硬件模糊测试方法，利用符号执行来实现卓越的覆盖范围。 SymbFuzz 是第一个在行业标准通用验证方法 (UVM) 上实施的硬件模糊测试技术，有助于无缝集成到商业硬件验证流程中。 SymbFuzz 在多种处理器 RTL 上进行了评估，包括 OpenTitan (Ibex)、CVA6、Rocket-Core 和 Mor1kx。这些设计涵盖了一系列处理器架构和复杂性。 SymbFuzz 检测到了现有模糊器之前发现的所有错误，还发现了 14 个新错误，其中包括 CWE 2025 数据库中报告的 OpenTitan 中的漏洞。与传统 UVM 随机测试相比，它还实现了高达 6.8 倍的更快收敛速度​​，与最先进的模糊器相比，实现了超过 2 × 10 4 的额外功能覆盖点，证明了其在改进跨不同处理器架构的 RTL 验证方面的有效性。

papers.title: Symbiotic Task Scheduling and Data Prefetching.
papers.abstract: Task-parallel programming models enable programmers to extract parallelism from irregular applications. Since software-based task-parallel runtimes impose crippling overheads on fine-grain tasks, architects have designed manycores with hardware support for task management. These hardware task-parallel systems can scale challenging workloads to hundreds of cores, but fail to use conventional prefetchers due to short (100-cycle) tasks. Lacking prefetching, they often expose DRAM latency to applications, fumbling the performance gains of hardware. We present the Task-Seeded Prefetcher ( TSP ) and Memory Response Task Scheduler ( MRS ), a symbiotic pair that boost performance in general-purpose task-parallel hardware. TSP learns and prefetches the data-access pattern of each task function, seeded with its descriptor that is queued by the task scheduler. MRS augments the baseline task-to-core dispatch policy by using prefetch status from TSP to optimize core utilization. Together, TSP and MRS provide speedups of up to 3.1 × (gmeans up to 1.4 ×) across 13 benchmarks on 256-core task-parallel systems that were already 3–60 × faster than parallel software.
papers.abstract_zh: 任务并行编程模型使程序员能够从不规则的应用程序中提取并行性。由于基于软件的任务并行运行时给细粒度任务带来了严重的开销，因此架构师设计了具有任务管理硬件支持的多核。这些硬件任务并行系统可以将具有挑战性的工作负载扩展到数百个核心，但由于任务较短（100 个周期）而无法使用传统的预取器。由于缺乏预取，它们经常会给应用程序带来 DRAM 延迟，从而影响硬件的性能提升。我们提出了任务种子预取器 ( TSP ) 和内存响应任务调度器 ( MRS )，这是一对可以提高通用任务并行硬件性能的共生对。 TSP 学习并预取每个任务函数的数据访问模式，并以其由任务调度程序排队的描述符作为种子。 MRS 通过使用 TSP 的预取状态来优化核心利用率，从而增强了基线任务到核心的调度策略。 TSP 和 MRS 共同在 256 核任务并行系统的 13 个基准测试中提供高达 3.1 倍（gmeans 高达 1.4 倍）的加速，而这些系统已经比并行软件快了 3-60 倍。

papers.title: TAIDL: Tensor Accelerator ISA Definition Language with Auto-generation of Scalable Test Oracles.
papers.abstract:
With the increasing importance of deep learning workloads, many hardware accelerators have been proposed in both academia and industry. However, software tooling for the vast majority of them does not exist compared to the software ecosystem and innovations proposed for established platforms such as CPUs and GPUs. We observed that the lack of well-defined hardware-software interfaces and correctness testing tools like fast and scalable test oracles (also known as functional simulators) act as significant barriers to adopting these emerging accelerators in the software community. These interfaces and tools are essential in building software such as retargetable compilers and optimized kernels.

To bridge these gaps, we first present TAIDL , an instruction specification language that provides novel constructs to describe the instruction set architectures (ISAs) of tensor accelerators. Next, given ISA definitions in TAIDL , we introduce techniques to automatically generate fast and scalable test oracles for diverse sets of accelerators, which are needed for testing software correctness of code that targets pre-silicon hardware designs. Automated generation of such tools reduces the burden on hardware architects and the repeated development efforts required across different accelerator platforms. Further, our techniques allow us to execute these simulators on GPUs, leading to highly scalable simulations. To demonstrate the expressivity of TAIDL , we instantiated several tensor accelerator ISAs with different compute capabilities and memory hierarchies. Further, we show that test oracles generated using TAIDL definitions are orders of magnitude faster and more scalable than existing instruction-level functional simulators, making them suitable for integration into software development cycles. TAIDL is available at https://github.com/act-compiler/taidl.
papers.abstract_zh:
随着深度学习工作负载的重要性日益增加，学术界和工业界都提出了许多硬件加速器。然而，与针对 CPU 和 GPU 等既定平台提出的软件生态系统和创新相比，其中绝大多数的软件工具并不存在。我们观察到，缺乏明确定义的硬件软件接口和正确性测试工具（例如快速且可扩展的测试预言机（也称为功能模拟器））是在软件社区中采用这些新兴加速器的重大障碍。这些接口和工具对于构建可重定向编译器和优化内核等软件至关重要。

为了弥补这些差距，我们首先提出 TAIDL，这是一种指令规范语言，它提供了新颖的结构来描述张量加速器的指令集架构 (ISA)。接下来，根据 TAIDL 中的 ISA 定义，我们介绍了为不同的加速器组自动生成快速且可扩展的测试预言机的技术，这些技术是测试针对硅前硬件设计的代码的软件正确性所必需的。此类工具的自动生成减轻了硬件架构师的负担，并减少了跨不同加速器平台所需的重复开发工作。此外，我们的技术允许我们在 GPU 上执行这些模拟器，从而实现高度可扩展的模拟。为了演示 TAIDL 的表达能力，我们实例化了几个具有不同计算能力和内存层次结构的张量加速器 ISA。此外，我们还表明，使用 TAIDL 定义生成的测试预言机比现有的指令级功能模拟器要快几个数量级，并且更具可扩展性，这使得它们适合集成到软件开发周期中。 TAIDL 可在 https://github.com/act-compiler/taidl 上获取。

papers.title: Titan-I: An Open-Source, High Performance RISC-V Vector Core.
papers.abstract: Vector processing has evolved from early systems like the CDC STAR-100 and Cray-1 to modern ISAs like ARM’s Scalable Vector Extension (SVE) and RISC-V Vector (RVV) extensions. However, scaling vector processing for contemporary workloads presents challenges due to overheads in traditional architectures. We introduce Titan-I (T1), an out-of-order (OoO) RVV architecture designed to scale both Instruction-Level Parallelism (ILP) and Data-Level Parallelism (DLP). T1 incorporates a coarse-grained floor-planning solver, a datapath-wide permutation unit, and a register cache for mask registers, improving bandwidth and reducing latency. It also uses multiple OoO techniques, such as fine-grained chaining, issue-as-commit and memory interleaving, to optimize ILP for wider vector datapaths. T1 outperforms General Purpose Graphic Processing Units (GP-GPU) and other vector cores, achieving up to 1.85 × speedup over GB202 (Nvidia 5090) and up to 2.41 × speedup over GA102 (Nvidia 3090) in cryptography with 40% of SM’s area of GA102. In HPC workloads, T1 offers a compatible performance with 19% area of HiSilicon TaiShan V120 (KP920), achieving a 4.59 × speedup over it when scaling T1’s datapath 4 ×. T1 also exhibits robust tolerance to memory latency, achieving an 8.05 × performance improvement over the SpacemiT X60 (K1) in pure DDR mode.
papers.abstract_zh: 矢量处理已从 CDC STAR-100 和 Cray-1 等早期系统发展到 ARM 的可扩展矢量扩展 (SVE) 和 RISC-V 矢量 (RVV) 扩展等现代 ISA。然而，由于传统架构中的开销，针对当代工作负载的扩展矢量处理提出了挑战。我们推出 Titan-I (T1)，这是一种无序 (OoO) RVV 架构，旨在扩展指令级并行性 (ILP) 和数据级并行性 (DLP)。 T1 包含一个粗粒度布局规划求解器、一个数据路径范围的排列单元和一个用于掩码寄存器的寄存器缓存，从而提高了带宽并减少了延迟。它还使用多种 OoO 技术，例如细粒度链接、提交时发出和内存交错，以优化 ILP 以实现更广泛的矢量数据路径。 T1 的性能优于通用图形处理单元 (GP-GPU) 和其他矢量核心，在加密方面比 GB202 (Nvidia 5090) 实现高达 1.85 倍的加速，比 GA102 (Nvidia 3090) 实现高达 2.41 倍的加速，SM 面积仅为 GA102 的 40%。在 HPC 工作负载中，T1 提供与海思 TaiShan V120 (KP920) 19% 面积兼容的性能，在将 T1 的数据路径扩展 4 倍时，实现了 4.59 倍的加速。 T1 还表现出对内存延迟的强大容忍度，在纯 DDR 模式下实现了比 SpacemiT X60 (K1) 8.05 倍的性能提升。

papers.title: Towards Closing the Performance Gap for Cryptographic Kernels Between CPUs and Specialized Hardware.
papers.abstract:
Specialized hardware like application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs) remains the primary accelerator type for cryptographic kernels based on large integer arithmetic. Prior work has shown that commodity and server-class GPUs can achieve near-ASIC performance for these workloads. However, achieving comparable performance on CPUs remains an open challenge. This work investigates the following question: How can we narrow the performance gap between CPUs and specialized hardware for key cryptographic kernels like basic linear algebra subprograms (BLAS) operations and the number theoretic transform (NTT)?

To this end, we develop an optimized scalar implementation of these kernels for x86 CPUs at the per-core level. We utilize SIMD instructions—specifically AVX2 and AVX-512—to further improve performance, achieving an average speedup of 38 times and 62 times over state-of-the-art CPU baselines for NTTs and BLAS operations, respectively. To narrow the gap further, we propose a small AVX-512 extension, dubbed multi-word extension (MQX), which delivers substantial speedup with only three new instructions and minimal proposed hardware modifications. MQX cuts the slowdown relative to ASICs to as low as 35 times on a single CPU core. Finally, we perform a roofline analysis to evaluate the peak performance achievable with MQX when scaled across an entire multi-core CPU. Our results show that, with MQX, top-tier server-grade CPUs can approach the performance of state-of-the-art ASICs for cryptographic workloads.
papers.abstract_zh:
专用集成电路 (ASIC) 等专用硬件仍然是基于大整数算术的加密内核的主要加速器类型。先前的研究表明，商用和服务器级 GPU 可以为这些工作负载实现接近 ASIC 的性能。然而，在 CPU 上实现可比较的性能仍然是一个开放的挑战。这项工作研究了以下问题：我们如何缩小 CPU 与关键加密内核（如基本线性代数子程序 (BLAS) 运算和数论变换 (NTT)）专用硬件之间的性能差距？

为此，我们在每个核心级别为 x86 CPU 开发了这些内核的优化标量实现。我们利用 SIMD 指令（特别是 AVX2 和 AVX-512）进一步提高性能，与最先进的 CPU 基准相比，NTT 和 BLAS 操作的平均加速分别提高了 38 倍和 62 倍。为了进一步缩小差距，我们提出了一个小型 AVX-512 扩展，称为多字扩展 (MQX)，它仅用三个新指令和最少的建议硬件修改即可提供显着的加速。 MQX 在单个 CPU 核心上将相对 ASIC 的速度降低至低至 35 倍。最后，我们执行屋顶线分析，以评估 MQX 在整个多核 CPU 上扩展时可实现的峰值性能。我们的结果表明，借助 MQX，顶级服务器级 CPU 可以在加密工作负载方面达到最先进 ASIC 的性能。

papers.title: TransFusion: End-to-End Transformer Acceleration via Graph Fusion and Pipelining.
papers.abstract: Transformer acceleration has increasingly emphasized local fusion within isolated submodules, such as multi-head attention (MHA) and softmax. However, as Transformer models continue to scale in both depth and context length, such fragmented optimizations fail to address end-to-end inefficiencies across the full encoder/decoder stack. This paper presents TransFusion, a comprehensive framework for end-to-end Transformer layers, including QKV projections, MHA, LayerNorm, and FFN, as structured Einsum Cascades, enabling precise modelling of data dependencies and execution order. TransFusion introduces DPipe, a unified graph-based scheduler that partitions the Einsum-centric directed acyclic graph (DAG) and applies latency-aware pipelining across hardware hierarchies using dynamic programming (DP). To enable scalable execution under strict memory budgets, TransFusion integrates TileSeek, a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)-based tiling search algorithm that balances buffer reuse and system constraints. Evaluated across both cloud and edge architecture, TransFusion achieves up to an average of 1.6 × speedup on cloud and 2.2 × on edge over the prior state-of-the-art, FuseMax, by jointly optimizing inter-layer data reuse, intra-layer pipelining, and operator scheduling.
papers.abstract_zh: Transformer 加速越来越强调孤立子模块内的局部融合，例如多头注意力 (MHA) 和 softmax。然而，随着 Transformer 模型在深度和上下文长度上不断扩展，这种碎片化的优化无法解决整个编码器/解码器堆栈中端到端的低效率问题。本文介绍了 TransFusion，这是一个端到端 Transformer 层的综合框架，包括 QKV 投影、MHA、LayerNorm 和 FFN，作为结构化 Einsum Cascades，能够对数据依赖性和执行顺序进行精确建模。 TransFusion 推出了 DPipe，这是一种基于图形的统一调度程序，它对以 Einsum 为中心的有向无环图 (DAG) 进行分区，并使用动态编程 (DP) 在硬件层次结构中应用延迟感知管道。为了在严格的内存预算下实现可扩展的执行，TransFusion 集成了 TileSeek，这是一种基于蒙特卡罗树搜索 (MCTS) 的平铺搜索算法，可平衡缓冲区重用和系统约束。通过云和边缘架构的评估，TransFusion 通过联合优化层间数据重用、层内流水线和算子调度，与之前最先进的 FuseMax 相比，在云上平均加速 1.6 倍，在边缘平均加速 2.2 倍。

papers.title: Understanding and Mitigating Covert Channel and Side Channel Vulnerabilities Introduced by RowHammer Defenses.
papers.abstract:
DRAM chips are increasingly vulnerable to read disturbance phenomena (e.g., RowHammer and RowPress), where repeatedly accessing or keeping open a DRAM row causes bitflips in nearby rows, due to DRAM density scaling. Attackers can exploit RowHammer bitflips in real systems to compromise security, which has motivated many prior works on RowHammer defenses. To enable such defenses, recent DDR specifications introduce new defense frameworks (e.g., PRAC and RFM). For robust (i.e., secure, safe, and reliable) operation , it is critical to analyze security implications of widely-adopted RowHammer defenses. Yet, no prior work analyzes the timing covert channel and side channel vulnerabilities RowHammer defenses introduce.

This paper presents the first analysis and evaluation of timing covert channel and side channel vulnerabilities introduced by state-of-the-art RowHammer defenses. We demonstrate that RowHammer defenses’ preventive actions (e.g., preventively refreshing potential victim rows) have two fundamental features that allow an attacker to exploit RowHammer defenses for timing leakage. First, preventive actions often reduce DRAM bandwidth availability because they block access to DRAM, thereby resulting in significantly longer memory access latencies. Second, users can intentionally trigger preventive actions because preventive actions highly depend on application memory access patterns.

We introduce LeakyHammer, a new class of attacks that leverage the RowHammer defense-induced memory latency differences to establish communication channels between processes and leak secrets from victim processes. First, we build two covert channel attacks exploiting two state-of-the-art RowHammer defenses (i.e., PRAC and RFM), achieving 39.0 Kbps and 48.7 Kbps channel capacity. Second, we demonstrate a proof-of-concept website fingerprinting attack that can identify visited websites based on the RowHammer-preventive actions they cause. We propose and evaluate three countermeasures against LeakyHammer. Our results show that fundamentally and completely mitigating LeakyHammer induces large performance overheads in highly RowHammer-vulnerable systems. We believe and hope our work can enable and aid future work on designing better solutions and more robust systems in the presence of such new vulnerabilities.
papers.abstract_zh:
DRAM 芯片越来越容易受到读取干扰现象（例如 RowHammer 和 RowPress）的影响，其中由于 DRAM 密度缩放，重复访问或保持打开 DRAM 行会导致附近行发生位翻转。攻击者可以在实际系统中利用 RowHammer 位翻转来破坏安全性，这激发了许多先前关于 RowHammer 防御的工作。为了实现此类防御，最近的 DDR 规范引入了新的防御框架（例如 PRAC 和 RFM）。为了实现稳健（即安全、可靠）的操作，分析广泛采用的 RowHammer 防御的安全影响至关重要。然而，之前没有任何工作分析 RowHammer 防御引入的定时隐蔽通道和侧通道漏洞。

本文首次对最先进的 RowHammer 防御引入的定时隐蔽通道和侧通道漏洞进行了分析和评估。我们证明 RowHammer 防御的预防措施（例如，预防性刷新潜在受害者行）具有两个基本特征，允许攻击者利用 RowHammer 防御进行定时泄漏。首先，预防措施通常会降低 DRAM 带宽可用性，因为它们会阻止对 DRAM 的访问，从而导致内存访问延迟显着延长。其次，用户可以有意触发预防性操作，因为预防性操作高度依赖于应用程序内存访问模式。

我们引入了 LeakyHammer，这是一种新型攻击，它利用 RowHammer 防御引起的内存延迟差异来建立进程之间的通信通道，并从受害者进程中泄漏秘密。首先，我们利用两种最先进的 RowHammer 防御（即 PRAC 和 RFM）构建两种隐蔽通道攻击，实现 39.0 Kbps 和 48.7 Kbps 的通道容量。其次，我们演示了一种概念验证网站指纹攻击，该攻击可以根据访问的网站引起的 RowHammer 预防操作来识别这些网站。我们提出并评估了针对 LeakyHammer 的三种对策。我们的结果表明，从根本上彻底缓解 LeakyHammer 会在 RowHammer 高度脆弱的系统中带来巨大的性能开销。我们相信并希望我们的工作能够促进并帮助未来在存在此类新漏洞的情况下设计更好的解决方案和更强大的系统。

papers.title: Vegapunk: Accurate and Fast Decoding for Quantum LDPC Codes with Online Hierarchical Algorithm and Sparse Accelerator.
papers.abstract: Quantum Low-Density Parity-Check (qLDPC) codes are a promising class of quantum error-correcting codes that exhibit constant-rate encoding and high error thresholds, thereby facilitating scalable fault-tolerant quantum computation. However, real-time decoding of qLDPC codes remains a significant challenge due to the high connectivity of their check matrices, which typically requires solving large-scale linear systems with sparse structures. In particular, off-the-shelf qLDPC decoders are often subject to a tradeoff between accuracy and latency, thus yielding no accurate and real-time decoding. This paper presents Vegapunk, a software-hardware co-design framework that enables real-time qLDPC decoding with high accuracy. To improve decoding accuracy, we design an offline decoupling strategy leveraging Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) optimizations to mitigate quantum degeneracy. To enable fast decoding, we introduce an online hierarchical decoding algorithm employing a greedy strategy. Furthermore, we show that our SMT-optimized strategy suffices to produce decoupled matrices with maximized sparsity, thus admitting a dedicated accelerator to fully exploit the sparsity and parallelism to achieve real-time qLDPC decoding. Experimental results demonstrate that Vegapunk enables real-time decoding (< 1 μs ) for the Bivariate Bicycle (BB) code up to [[784,24,24]] while exhibiting logical error rates on par with the state-of-the-art decoder, i.e., BP+OSD.
papers.abstract_zh: 量子低密度奇偶校验（qLDPC）码是一类很有前途的量子纠错码，它具有恒定速率编码和高错误阈值，从而促进可扩展的容错量子计算。然而，由于其校验矩阵的高连通性，qLDPC 码的实时解码仍然是一个重大挑战，这通常需要求解具有稀疏结构的大规模线性系统。特别是，现成的 qLDPC 解码器通常需要在准确性和延迟之间进行权衡，从而无法实现准确和实时的解码。本文提出了 Vegapunk，一种软硬件协同设计框架，可实现高精度的实时 qLDPC 解码。为了提高解码精度，我们设计了一种离线解耦策略，利用可满足性模理论（SMT）优化来减轻量子简并性。为了实现快速解码，我们引入了一种采用贪婪策略的在线分层解码算法。此外，我们表明，我们的 SMT 优化策略足以产生具有最大化稀疏性的解耦矩阵，从而允许专用加速器充分利用稀疏性和并行性来实现实时 qLDPC 解码。实验结果表明，Vegapunk 能够对高达 [[784,24,24]] 的双变量 Bicycle (BB) 代码进行实时解码（< 1 μs），同时表现出与最先进的解码器（即 BP+OSD）相当的逻辑错误率。

papers.title: X-SET: An Efficient Graph Pattern Matching Accelerator With Order-Aware Parallel Intersection Units.
papers.abstract:
Graph Pattern Matching (GPM) is a critical task in a wide range of graph analytics applications, such as social network analysis and cybersecurity. Despite its importance, GPM remains challenging to accelerate due to its inherently irregular control flow and heavy reliance on set operations, which dominate execution time and introduce data dependencies that limit parallelism. While recent GPM accelerators attempt to improve performance, they often overlook the ordered nature of input data, resulting in redundant computations and inefficient hardware utilization.

This paper presents X-SET, a GPM accelerator that overcomes these limitations by introducing two key innovations. First, we propose an Order-Aware Set Intersection Unit (SIU), which exploits input ordering to reduce the hardware complexity of parallel set intersection from \(\mathcal {O}(N^2)\) to \(\mathcal {O}(N \log N)\) , achieving high throughput and significant area savings by avoiding unnecessary comparisons. Second, we develop a barrier-free task scheduler that breaks traditional DFS scheduling constraints by enabling asynchronous, out-of-order task execution across different levels of the GPM search tree. X-SET is integrated into a RISC-V SoC, supporting end-to-end acceleration. Extensive experimental results show that X-SET outperforms state-of-the-art GPM accelerators, achieving 4.6 × - 142.9 × improvements in compute density, with a geometric mean of 13.7 × , and delivering 6.4 × geometric mean and 42.9 × maximum speedup in end-to-end performance. X-SET is open-sourced at github 1 .
papers.abstract_zh:
图模式匹配（GPM）是社交网络分析和网络安全等各种图分析应用中的一项关键任务。尽管 GPM 很重要，但由于其固有的不规则控制流和对集合操作的严重依赖，GPM 仍然难以加速，而集合操作主导了执行时间并引入了限制并行性的数据依赖性。虽然最近的 GPM 加速器试图提高性能，但它们经常忽视输入数据的有序性质，从而导致冗余计算和低效的硬件利用率。

本文介绍了 X-SET，这是一种 GPM 加速器，它通过引入两项关键创新来克服这些限制。首先，我们提出了一种顺序感知集交集单元（SIU），它利用输入排序来降低并行集交集的硬件复杂性，从 \(\mathcal {O}(N^2)\) 到 \(\mathcal {O}(N \log N)\) ，通过避免不必要的比较来实现高吞吐量和显着节省面积。其次，我们开发了一种无障碍任务调度程序，它通过在 GPM 搜索树的不同级别上实现异步、无序任务执行来打破传统的 DFS 调度约束。 X-SET集成到RISC-V SoC中，支持端到端加速。大量实验结果表明，X-SET 的性能优于最先进的 GPM 加速器，计算密度实现了 4.6 × - 142.9 × 的提升，几何平均值为 13.7 × ，端到端性能实现了 6.4 × 几何平均值和 42.9 × 最大加速。 X-SET 在 github 1 上开源。

papers.title: YOUTIAO: Hybrid Multiplexing with Dynamic Qubit Grouping for Low-cost and Scalable Quantum Wiring.
papers.abstract: With continuous advances in physical technology, the number of qubits has increased from just a few to several thousand. To further extend the scale, the density of control lines has become one of the major limitations that decides the cost and control fidelity. Specifically, each superconducting qubit requires dedicated control lines to manipulate its state; however, these lines reach the spatial upper limit of the cryostat when scaling up the quantum chip. Inspired by collinear signal transmission, a promising solution is to adopt multiplexing methods—such as frequency-division multiplexing (FDM) and time-division multiplexing (TDM)—to share control lines among superconducting qubits. However, existing methods lack a systematic architectural approach to support multiplexing-aware wiring, which leads to low parallelism and high crosstalk during deployment. In this work, we propose a multiplexing-aware design for the peripheral control lines of superconducting quantum processors, combining cryostat-level wiring optimization with on-chip routing. Our key novelty lies in a hybrid multiplexing architecture that adopts FDM for XY control and readout lines, and TDM for Z control lines. This enables high utilization of natural non-parallel operations, thereby cutting the additional circuit depth for TDM control. Finally, we develop an interaction model that co-optimizes qubit layout and multiplexed channel allocation. Our experiments use the data collected from self-developed Xmon quantum chips. The results show that YOUTIAO achieves a 67.7% reduction in cryostat-level coaxial wiring complexity and overall superconducting quantum system costs, while reducing on-chip routing area by 23%. With these improvements, we still keep the 1q-gate fidelity at 99.98%, and only introduce 5% extra circuit latency compared to the partial-multiplexing system.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着物理技术的不断进步，量子比特的数量已经从几个增加到几千个。为了进一步扩大规模，控制线的密度已成为决定成本和控制保真度的主要限制之一。具体来说，每个超导量子位都需要专用的控制线来操纵其状态；然而，当按比例放大量子芯片时，这些线达到了低温恒温器的空间上限。受共线信号传输的启发，一种有前景的解决方案是采用频分复用 (FDM) 和时分复用 (TDM) 等复用方法，在超导量子位之间共享控制线。然而，现有方法缺乏系统的架构方法来支持多路复用感知布线，这导致部署期间的低并行性和高串扰。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种针对超导量子处理器的外围控制线的多路复用感知设计，将低温恒温器级布线优化与片上布线相结合。我们的主要创新点在于混合多路复用架构，该架构采用 FDM 进行 XY 控制和读出线，采用 TDM 进行 Z 控制线。这使得自然非并行操作的高利用率成为可能，从而减少了用于 TDM 控制的额外电路深度。最后，我们开发了一种交互模型，可以共同优化量子位布局和多路复用通道分配。我们的实验使用的是自主研发的Xmon量子芯片收集的数据。结果表明，油条实现了低温恒温器级同轴布线复杂性和整体超导量子系统成本降低67.7%，同时片上布线面积减少23%。通过这些改进，我们仍然将 1q 门保真度保持在 99.98%，并且与部分复用系统相比仅引入了 5% 的额外电路延迟。

papers.title: ρHammer: Reviving RowHammer Attacks on New Architectures via Prefetching.
papers.abstract: Rowhammer is a critical vulnerability in dynamic random access memory (DRAM) that continues to pose a significant threat to various systems. However, we find that conventional load-based attacks are becoming highly ineffective on the most recent architectures such as Intel Alder and Raptor Lake. In this paper, we present ρ Hammer, a new Rowhammer framework that systematically overcomes three core challenges impeding attacks on these new architectures. First, we design an efficient and generic DRAM address mapping reverse-engineering method that uses selective pairwise measurements and structured deduction, enabling recovery of complex mappings within seconds on the latest memory controllers. Second, to break through the activation rate bottleneck of load-based hammering, we introduce a novel prefetch-based hammering paradigm that leverages the asynchronous nature of x86 prefetch instructions and is further enhanced by multi-bank parallelism to maximize throughput. Third, recognizing that speculative execution causes more severe disorder issues for prefetching, which cannot be simply mitigated by memory barriers, we develop a counter-speculation hammering technique using control-flow obfuscation and optimized NOP -based pseudo-barriers to maintain prefetch order with minimal overhead. Evaluations across four latest Intel architectures demonstrate ρ Hammer’s breakthrough effectiveness: it induces up to 200K+ additional bit flips within 2-hour attack pattern fuzzing processes and has a 112× higher flip rate than the load-based hammering baselines on Comet and Rocket Lake. Also, we are the first to revive Rowhammer attacks on the latest Raptor Lake architecture, where baselines completely fail, achieving stable flip rates of 2,291/min and fast end-to-end exploitation.
papers.abstract_zh: Rowhammer 是动态随机存取存储器 (DRAM) 中的一个严重漏洞，持续对各种系统构成重大威胁。然而，我们发现传统的基于负载的攻击在最新的架构（例如 Intel Alder 和 Raptor Lake）上变得非常无效。在本文中，我们提出了 ρ Hammer，这是一种新的 Rowhammer 框架，它系统地克服了阻碍对这些新架构进行攻击的三个核心挑战。首先，我们设计了一种高效且通用的 DRAM 地址映射逆向工程方法，该方法使用选择性成对测量和结构化推导，从而能够在最新的内存控制器上在几秒钟内恢复复杂的映射。其次，为了突破基于负载的锤击的激活率瓶颈，我们引入了一种新颖的基于预取的锤击范例，该范例利用 x86 预取指令的异步特性，并通过多存储体并行性进一步增强，以最大化吞吐量。第三，认识到推测执行会导致更严重的预取混乱问题，而这不能简单地通过内存屏障来缓解，我们开发了一种反推测锤击技术，使用控制流混淆和优化的基于 NOP 的伪屏障，以最小的开销维持预取顺序。对四种最新英特尔架构的评估证明了 ρ Hammer 的突破性有效性：它在 2 小时的攻击模式模糊处理过程中引发了多达 200K+ 的额外位翻转，并且翻转率比 Comet 和 Rocket Lake 上基于负载的锤击基线高出 112 倍。此外，我们是第一个在最新的 Raptor Lake 架构上恢复 Rowhammer 攻击的人，该架构的基线完全失败，实现了 2,291/分钟的稳定翻转率和快速的端到端利用。

papers.title: A Case for Speculative Address Translation with Rapid Validation for GPUs.
papers.abstract: A unified address space is vital for heterogeneous systems as it enables efficient data sharing between CPUs and GPUs. However, GPU address translation faces challenges due to high TLB pressure, particularly with irregular and memory-intensive applications. Compared to an ideal scenario, we observe that address translation overheads cause a slowdown of up to 34.5% in modern heterogeneous systems. This paper introduces Avatar, a novel framework to accelerate address translation in GPUs. Avatar comprises two key components: Contiguity-Aware Speculative Translation (CAST) and In-Cache Validation (CAVA) mechanisms. Avatar identifies the potential for predicting virtual-to-physical address mapping by monitoring contiguous pages that lie in both virtual and physical address spaces. Leveraging this insight, CAST speculatively translates virtual addresses into physical addresses. This speculative address translation enables immediate data fetching into GPUs while addressing translation occurs in the background, reducing TLB-miss overhead. Unfortunately, modern GPUs lack support for speculative execution, which limits CAST's performance gain. Data fetched from speculated physical addresses is unusable until validation. CAVA addresses this limitation by quickly validating speculated physical addresses. To this end, CAVA embeds page mapping information into each 32B sector of 128B cache lines. Thus, CAVA enables fetching a sector block from memory for a speculated address and rapidly validating the speculative translation using the embedded mapping information. Our experiments show that Avatar achieves a 90.3% (high) speculation accuracy and improves GPU performance by 37.2% (on average).
papers.abstract_zh: 统一的地址空间对于异构系统至关重要，因为它可以实现 CPU 和 GPU 之间的高效数据共享。然而，由于 TLB 压力较高，GPU 地址转换面临挑战，特别是对于不规则和内存密集型应用程序。与理想场景相比，我们观察到地址转换开销导致现代异构系统速度下降高达 34.5%。本文介绍了 Avatar，这是一种加速 GPU 地址转换的新颖框架。 Avatar 包含两个关键组件：连续感知推测翻译 (CAST) 和缓存内验证 (CAVA) 机制。 Avatar 通过监视位于虚拟和物理地址空间中的连续页面来识别预​​测虚拟到物理地址映射的潜力。利用这种洞察力，CAST 推测性地将虚拟地址转换为物理地址。这种推测性地址转换可以立即将数据提取到 GPU 中，同时地址转换在后台进行，从而减少了 TLB 未命中开销。不幸的是，现代 GPU 缺乏对推测执行的支持，这限制了 CAST 的性能增益。从推测的物理地址获取的数据在验证之前无法使用。 CAVA 通过快速验证推测的物理地址来解决此限制。为此，CAVA将页面映射信息嵌入到128B缓存行的每个32B扇区中。因此，CAVA 能够从内存中获取推测地址的扇区块，并使用嵌入的映射信息快速验证推测的转换。我们的实验表明，Avatar 的推测准确率达到了 90.3%（高），GPU 性能提高了 37.2%（平均）。

papers.title: A Compiler-Like Framework for Optimizing Cryptographic Big Integer Multiplication on GPUs.
papers.abstract: With the growth of digital data and rising security concerns, techniques for privacy-preserving computation have become increasingly essential. Big integer multiplication, pivotal for these applications, is compute-intensive but poses challenges for GPU acceleration due to its complexity and the need for application-specific tailored implementations. This paper presents IMCompiler, a compiler-like framework that automatically gen-erates optimized GPU kernels for integer multiplications used in cryptosystems. It features a frontend-IR-backend structure, where the Intermediate Representation (IR) employs a segmented integer multiplication algorithm to decouple architecture-specific optimizations from high-level parameters. The frontend can then easily translate integer multiplication with various high-level parameters into the IR, while the backend focuses on fine-tuning a single GPU kernel for each device, enabling automatic code generation. Moreover, we introduce a computation diagram to facilitate the analysis of parallelization strategies, inspiring many optimizations, including two-dimensional parallelization, tailored caching strategy, index transposing, and lazy carrying. Experiments show that IMCompiler achieves a 4.47× speedup compared to the widely used baseline and 1.42 × over Nvidia's official library. The speedup will be even higher for larger integers and higher-capacity GPUs.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着数字数据的增长和安全问题的日益严重，隐私保护计算技术变得越来越重要。对于这些应用程序来说至关重要的大整数乘法是计算密集型的，但由于其复杂性和需要针对特定​​应用程序定制实现，给 GPU 加速带来了挑战。本文介绍了 IMCompiler，这是一个类似编译器的框架，可以自动为密码系统中使用的整数乘法生成优化的 GPU 内核。它具有前端-IR-后端结构，其中中间表示 (IR) 采用分段整数乘法算法将特定于架构的优化与高级参数解耦。然后，前端可以轻松地将具有各种高级参数的整数乘法转换为 IR，而后端则专注于为每个设备微调单个 GPU 内核，从而实现自动代码生成。此外，我们引入了计算图来方便分析并行化策略，启发许多优化，包括二维并行化、定制缓存策略、索引转置和惰性携带。实验表明，与广泛使用的基准相比，IMCompiler 实现了 4.47 倍的加速，比 Nvidia 官方库实现了 1.42 倍的加速。对于更大的整数和更高容量的 GPU，加速会更高。

papers.title: A Framework for Fine-Grained Program Versioning.
papers.abstract: Static dependence analysis is critical for optimizations such as vectorization and loop-invariant code motion. However, traditional static dependence analysis is often imprecise, making these optimizations less effective. To address this issue, production compilers use loop versioning to rule out some categories of memory dependencies at run time. However, loop versioning is loop-centric and usually tied to specific optimizations (e.g., loop vectorization), making it less effective for non-loop optimizations such as superword-level parallelism (SLP) vectorization. In this paper, we propose a fine-grained versioning framework to rule out program dependencies at run time. Our framework is general and not tailored to any specific optimizations. To use our system, a client optimization specifies groups of instructions (or loops) whose independence is desired but unprovable statically. In response, our system duplicates the appropriate instructions and guards the original ones with run-time checks to guarantee their independence; if the checks fail, the duplicated instructions execute instead. In a case study, we extended an existing SLP vectorizer with minimal modifications using our framework, resulting in a 1.17× speedup over Clang's vectorizers on TSVC and a 1.51× speedup on PolyBench. In both benchmarks, we encountered programs that could not be vectorized with loop versioning alone. In a second case study, we used our framework to implement a more aggressive variant of redundant load elimination than the one implemented by Clang. Our redundant load elimination results in a 1.012× speedup on the SPEC 2017 Floating Point benchmarks, with the maximum speedup being 1.064×.
papers.abstract_zh: 静态相关性分析对于矢量化和循环不变代码运动等优化至关重要。然而，传统的静态依赖分析通常不精确，使得这些优化效果较差。为了解决这个问题，生产编译器使用循环版本控制来排除运行时某些类别的内存依赖性。然而，循环版本控制是以循环为中心的，通常与特定的优化（例如循环向量化）相关，这使得它对于非循环优化（例如超字级并行（SLP）向量化）效果较差。在本文中，我们提出了一个细粒度的版本控制框架来排除运行时的程序依赖性。我们的框架是通用的，并不针对任何特定的优化。为了使用我们的系统，客户端优化指定了一组指令（或循环），其独立性是期望的，但静态地无法证明。作为回应，我们的系统复制适当的指令，并通过运行时检查来保护原始指令，以保证它们的独立性；如果检查失败，则执行重复的指令。在一个案例研究中，我们使用我们的框架以最小的修改扩展了现有的 SLP 矢量化器，在 TSVC 上比 Clang 的矢量化器实现了 1.17 倍的加速，在 PolyBench 上实现了 1.51 倍的加速。在这两个基准测试中，我们都遇到了无法仅通过循环版本控制进行矢量化的程序。在第二个案例研究中，我们使用我们的框架实现了一种比 Clang 实现的更激进的冗余负载消除变体。我们的冗余负载消除在 SPEC 2017 浮点基准测试中实现了 1.012 倍的加速，最大加速为 1.064 倍。

papers.title: A Mess of Memory System Benchmarking, Simulation and Application Profiling.
papers.abstract: The Memory stress (Mess) framework provides a unified view of the memory system benchmarking, simulation and application profiling. The Mess benchmark provides a holistic and detailed memory system characterization. It is based on hundreds of measurements that are represented as a family of bandwidth-latency curves. The benchmark increases the coverage of all the previous tools and leads to new findings in the behavior of the actual and simulated memory systems. We deploy the Mess benchmark to characterize Intel, AMD, IBM, Fujitsu, Amazon and NVIDIA servers with DDR4, DDR5, HBM2 and HBM2E memory. The Mess memory simulator uses bandwidth-latency concept for the memory performance simulation. We integrate Mess with widely-used CPUs simulators enabling modeling of all high-end memory technologies. The Mess simulator is fast, easy to integrate and it closely matches the actual system performance. By design, it enables a quick adoption of new memory technologies in hardware simulators. Finally, the Mess application profiling positions the application in the bandwidth-latency space of the target memory system. This information can be correlated with other application runtime activities and the source code, leading to a better overall understanding of the application's behavior. The current Mess benchmark release covers all major CPU and GPU ISAs, x86, ARM, Power, RISC-V, and NVIDIA's PTX. We also release as open source the ZSim, gem5 and OpenPiton Metro-MPI integrated with the Mess simulator for DDR4, DDR5, Optane, HBM2, HBM2E and CXL memory expanders. The Mess application profiling is already integrated into a suite of production HPC performance analysis tools.
papers.abstract_zh: 内存压力 (Mess) 框架提供了内存系统基准测试、模拟和应用程序分析的统一视图。 Mess 基准测试提供了全面且详细的内存系统特征。它基于数百个测量结果，这些测量结果表示为一系列带宽延迟曲线。该基准测试增加了所有先前工具的覆盖范围，并导致了实际和模拟内存系统行为的新发现。我们部署 Mess 基准测试来表征配备 DDR4、DDR5、HBM2 和 HBM2E 内存的 Intel、AMD、IBM、Fujitsu、Amazon 和 NVIDIA 服务器。 Mess 内存模拟器使用带宽延迟概念进行内存性能模拟。我们将 Mess 与广泛使用的 CPU 模拟器集成，从而能够对所有高端内存技术进行建模。 Mess 模拟器速度快、易于集成，并且与实际系统性能紧密匹配。通过设计，它可以在硬件模拟器中快速采用新的内存技术。最后，Mess 应用程序分析将应用程序定位在目标内存系统的带宽延迟空间中。该信息可以与其他应用程序运行时活动和源代码相关联，从而更好地全面了解应用程序的行为。当前的 Mess 基准测试版本涵盖所有主要 CPU 和 GPU ISA、x86、ARM、Power、RISC-V 和 NVIDIA 的 PTX。我们还开源了 ZSim、gem5 和 OpenPiton Metro-MPI，它们与适用于 DDR4、DDR5、Optane、HBM2、HBM2E 和 CXL 内存扩展器的 Mess 模拟器集成。 Mess 应用程序分析已集成到一套生产 HPC 性能分析工具中。

papers.title: A Scalable, Efficient, and Robust Dynamic Memory Management Library for HLS-based FPGAs.
papers.abstract: Nowadays, high-level synthesis (HLS) has gained prominence for FPGA-based architecture prototyping, enhancing productivity significantly. Despite this advancement, HLS tools are impeded by a critical drawback: they lack support for dynamic memory management (DMM), leading to static mem-ory allocation and suboptimal use of memory resources. In response, numerous efforts have been made to develop DMM solutions compatible with HLS. However, our analysis indicates that existing solutions fail to concurrently meet the desired trifecta of scalability (efficient management of memory of any size), efficiency (minimal latency in memory (de-)allocation), and robustness (low allocation failure rates). This limitation hampers their applicability in real-world scenarios. In this paper, we introduce GraDMM, a “three-birds-one- stone” solution that comprehensively enhances the scalability, efficiency, and robustness of DMM. The key insight is to formulate memory (de-)allocation as graph analytics and lever-age sophisticated FPGA-based graph processing techniques. To achieve scalability, GraDMM specializes a simplified pipeline that significantly suppresses resource utilization expansion caused by managed memory scaling. This is crucial for managing arbitrarily sized memory on resource-limited FPGA platforms. For efficiency, GraDMM implements a data-centric concurrent traversal scheme and a shortcut-assisted fast traversal policy to accelerate (de-)allocation-guided graph traversal, reducing mem-ory (de-)allocation latency. To enhance robustness, GraDMM incorporates an adaptive memory defragmenter that defragments managed memory to minimize fragmentation-induced allocation failures. GraDMM is encapsulated as a library, providing high- level interfaces for users and ensuring synthesizability with Vi- vado HLS. Experimental results demonstrate that GraDMM out-performs three state-of-the-art HLS-compatible DMM solutions by significant margins: 56.71 %-85.59% in resource consumption savings, 78.94 % -99.99 % in (de-)allocation latency improvement, and 10.71 %-65.75% in allocation failure reduction.
papers.abstract_zh: 如今，高级综合 (HLS) 在基于 FPGA 的架构原型设计中占据了重要地位，显着提高了生产效率。尽管取得了这一进步，HLS 工具仍受到一个严重缺陷的阻碍：它们缺乏对动态内存管理 (DMM) 的支持，导致静态内存分配和内存资源的次优使用。为此，人们做出了大量努力来开发与 HLS 兼容的 DMM 解决方案。然而，我们的分析表明，现有的解决方案无法同时满足可扩展性（任何大小的内存的有效管理）、效率（内存（取消）分配的最小延迟）和稳健性（低分配失败率）所需的三重优势。这种限制阻碍了它们在现实场景中的适用性。在本文中，我们介绍了GraDMM，一种“三鸟一石”的解决方案，全面增强了DMM的可扩展性、效率和鲁棒性。关键见解是将内存（取消）分配制定为图形分析并利用复杂的基于 FPGA 的图形处理技术。为了实现可扩展性，GraDMM 专门设计了一种简化的管道，可以显着抑制托管内存扩展导致的资源利用率扩展。这对于在资源有限的 FPGA 平台上管理任意大小的内存至关重要。为了提高效率，GraDMM 实现了以数据为中心的并发遍历方案和快捷方式辅助的快速遍历策略，以加速（取消）分配引导的图遍历，减少内存（取消）分配延迟。为了增强鲁棒性，GraDMM 结合了一个自适应内存碎片整理程序，可以对托管内存进行碎片整理，以最大程度地减少碎片引起的分配失败。 GraDMM 被封装为一个库，为用户提供高级接口并确保与 Vivado HLS 的可综合性。实验结果表明，GraDMM 的性能显着优于三种最先进的 HLS 兼容 DMM 解决方案：资源消耗节省 56.71 %-85.59%，（取消）分配延迟改善 78.94 % -99.99 %，分配失败减少 10.71 %-65.75%。

papers.title: Acamar: A Dynamically Reconfigurable Scientific Computing Accelerator for Robust Convergence and Minimal Resource Underutilization.
papers.abstract: Although modern supercomputers are capable of delivering Exaflops now, they do not always achieve their peak performance. For instance, even today's high-end supercom-puters achieve only less than 5% of their peak FLOPS when running HPCG, a benchmark designed to represent real-world scientific computing programs. To improve the efficiency of the key kernels in scientific computing, such as those used in solving partial differential equations, computer architects have begun to expand the applications of domain-specific architectures (DSAs) to scientific computing. However, DSAs that often have a fixed design are not likely to be practical solutions, as one specialized solution cannot fit all the diverse scientific computing workloads, making them less effective. The challenges of hardware inef-ficiency in today's supercomputers and the ineffectiveness of DSAs are further exacerbated by sparsity, a key characteristic of scientific computing workloads. While prior studies have proposed DSA solutions for sparse computations, they too are static and not adaptable to variations in the patterns and levels of sparsity across different scientific workloads. To address these challenges and target not only the diversity of computations in such workloads but also variations in sparsity, we propose Acamar 1 1 Acamar /’ rckomarr/ is a binary star system in the constellation of Eridanus., a dynamically reconfigurable accelerator. Acamar is adaptable to various solvers across different workloads and dynamically optimizes the trade-off between resource utilization and latency for sparse computations. The adaptable design also enables selecting a solver that guarantees convergence. We evaluate Acamar based on its Vitis HLS implementation on Xilinx Alveo u55c. Our experiments show a resource utilization and latency improvement up to 3.5 x and 6 x as well as improved performance efficiency and achieved throughput over a static design and Nvidia GTX 1650 Super.
papers.abstract_zh: 尽管现代超级计算机现在能够提供 Exaflops，但它们并不总能达到峰值性能。例如，即使今天的高端超级计算机在运行 HPCG（旨在代表现实世界科学计算程序的基准）时也只能实现不到峰值 FLOPS 的 5%。为了提高科学计算中关键内核（例如用于求解偏微分方程的内核）的效率，计算机架构师已经开始将特定领域体系结构（DSA）的应用扩展到科学计算。然而，通常具有固定设计的 DSA 不太可能成为实用的解决方案，因为一种专门的解决方案无法满足所有不同的科学计算工作负载，从而降低其效率。科学计算工作负载的一个关键特征——稀疏性进一步加剧了当今超级计算机中硬件效率低下和 DSA 效率低下的挑战。虽然之前的研究提出了用于稀疏计算的 DSA 解决方案，但它们也是静态的，无法适应不同科学工作负载的稀疏模式和水平的变化。为了应对这些挑战，不仅针对此类工作负载中计算的多样性，还针对稀疏性的变化，我们提出了 Acamar 1 1 Acamar /’ rckomarr/ 是波江座的一个双星系统，是一个动态可重构加速器。 Acamar 可适应不同工作负载的各种求解器，并动态优化稀疏计算的资源利用率和延迟之间的权衡。适应性设计还可以选择保证收敛的求解器。我们基于 Xilinx Alveo u55c 上的 Vitis HLS 实施来评估 Acamar。我们的实验表明，与静态设计和 Nvidia GTX 1650 Super 相比，资源利用率和延迟分别提高了 3.5 倍和 6 倍，并且提高了性能效率并实现了吞吐量。

papers.title: Accelerating Zero-Knowledge Proofs Through Hardware-Algorithm Co-Design.
papers.abstract: Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) are a cryptographic tool that enables one party (a prover) to prove to another (a verifier) that a statement is true, without requiring the prover to disclose any data to the verifier. ZKPs have many use cases, such as letting clients delegate computation to servers with cryptographic correctness guarantees, while enabling the server to use secret data in these computations. ZKP applications span verifiable machine learning (ML) and databases, online auctions, electronic voting, and blockchains. While ZKPs are already widely used in blockchains, the prohibitive costs of proof generation limit them to proving very simple computations. We present a novel accelerator, NoCap, that leverages hardware-algorithm co-design to achieve transformative speedups. NoCap generates proofs 586× faster than a 32-core CPU, and 41× faster than PipeZK, a state-of-the-art ZKP accelerator. We leverage recent algorithmic developments to achieve these speedups: we identify and combine two recent hash-based ZKP algorithms, Orion and Spartan, which have similar performance on CPUs to the ZKPs targeted by prior accelerators, but are much more amenable to hardware acceleration. Though these algorithms result in larger proofs, we show that the end-to-end speedups (including prover time, proof transmission, and verification time) more than justify this size increase. We contribute a novel hardware organization to exploit these acceleration opportunities: NoCap is a programmable vector processor with functional units tailored to the needs of hash-based ZKPs. We also contribute a co-designed implementation of the Spartan+Orion ZKP tailored to accelerators, with optimizations that improve parallelism and reduce memory traffic. As a result, NoCap achieves speedups that enable new use cases for ZKP.
papers.abstract_zh: 零知识证明 (ZKP) 是一种加密工具，使一方（证明者）能够向另一方（验证者）证明某个陈述是真实的，而不要求证明者向验证者透露任何数据。 ZKP 有许多用例，例如让客户端将计算委托给具有加密正确性保证的服务器，同时使服务器能够在这些计算中使用秘密数据。 ZKP 应用涵盖可验证的机器学习 (ML) 和数据库、在线拍卖、电子投票和区块链。虽然 ZKP 已经在区块链中广泛使用，但证明生成的高昂成本限制了它们只能证明非常简单的计算。我们提出了一种新颖的加速器 NoCap，它利用硬件算法协同设计来实现变革性的加速。 NoCap 生成证明的速度比 32 核 CPU 快 586 倍，比最先进的 ZKP 加速器 PipeZK 快 41 倍。我们利用最新的算法开发来实现这些加速：我们识别并结合了两种最新的基于哈希的 ZKP 算法 Orion 和 Spartan，它们在 CPU 上的性能与先前加速器针对的 ZKP 相似，但更适合硬件加速。尽管这些算法会产生更大的证明，但我们表明端到端加速（包括证明者时间、证明传输和验证时间）足以证明这种规模增加是合理的。我们贡献了一种新颖的硬件组织来利用这些加速机会：NoCap 是一种可编程矢量处理器，其功能单元适合基于哈希的 ZKP 的需求。我们还贡献了针对加速器量身定制的 Spartan+Orion ZKP 的共同设计实现，并进行了可提高并行性并减少内存流量的优化。因此，NoCap 实现了加速，为 ZKP 提供了新的用例。

papers.title: ActiveN: A Scalable and Flexibly-Programmable Event-Driven Neuromorphic Processor.
papers.abstract: At present, most neuromorphic chips utilize custom circuits and/or on-chip memory to achieve neural computations and parameter storage. However, with the diversified development of brain-inspired applications, this approach faces significant challenges regarding programming flexibility and scalability. To address these issues, we propose a RISC-V-based many-core neuromorphic architecture, ActiveN. Each neuro-core is equipped with an active-message-enabled micro-architecture to support the event-driven programming model of Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs), and the memory subsystem is enhanced to identify sparse data and forward them directly. These mechanisms significantly mitigate the impact of memory latency on performance to enable the storage of synapse data in off-chip (or off-die) bulk storages (e.g., DRAMs, HBMs), which not only improve storage scalability but also increase computing density. Furthermore, the core's instruction set is customized to include a compact and complete set of fixed-and floating-point computing instructions, supporting various neural models and SNN computation algorithms flexibly. End-to-end prototype testing demonstrates that, compared to a state-of-the-art chip based on custom circuitry and on-chip SRAM that also supports event-driven operations, ActiveN can integrate over 10x more processing units (512) with strong scalability to fully utilize memory bandwidth. Additionally, it achieves 7.9 times the performance of this counterpart and 96.6 times the performance of an NVIDIA A100 GPU, while maintaining flexible programmability.
papers.abstract_zh: 目前，大多数神经形态芯片利用定制电路和/或片上存储器来实现神经计算和参数存储。然而，随着类脑应用的多元化发展，这种方法在编程灵活性和可扩展性方面面临着巨大的挑战。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种基于 RISC-V 的多核神经形态架构 ActiveN。每个神经核心都配备了支持活动消息的微架构，以支持尖峰神经网络（SNN）的事件驱动编程模型，并且内存子系统得到增强，可以识别稀疏数据并直接转发它们。这些机制显着减轻了内存延迟对性能的影响，从而能够将突触数据存储在片外（或芯片外）大容量存储（例如 DRAM、HBM）中，这不仅提高了存储可扩展性，还提高了计算密度。此外，该内核指令集经过定制，包含一套紧凑而完整的定点和浮点计算指令，灵活支持各种神经模型和SNN计算算法。端到端原型测试表明，与基于定制电路和支持事件驱动操作的片上 SRAM 的最先进芯片相比，ActiveN 可以集成超过 10 倍的处理单元 (512)，并且具有强大的可扩展性，以充分利用内存带宽。此外，它的性能是同类产品的 7.9 倍，是 NVIDIA A100 GPU 性能的 96.6 倍，同时保持灵活的可编程性。

papers.title: AdapTiV: Sign-Similarity Based Image-Adaptive Token Merging for Vision Transformer Acceleration.
papers.abstract: The advent of Vision Transformers (ViT) has set a new performance leap in computer vision by leveraging self-attention mechanisms. However, the computational efficiency of ViTs is limited by the quadratic complexity of self-attention and redundancy among image tokens. To address these issues, token merging strategies have been explored to reduce input size by merging similar tokens. Nonetheless, implementing token merging presents a degradation of latency performance due to its two factors: inefficient computations and fixed merge rate nature. This paper introduces AdapTiV, a novel hardware-software co-designed accelerator that accelerates ViTs through image-adaptive token merging, effectively addressing the afore-mentioned challenges. Under the design philosophy of reducing the overhead of token merging and concealing its latency within the Layer Normalization (LN) process, AdapTiV incorporates algorithmic innovations such as Local Matching, which restricts the search space for token merging, thereby reducing the computational complexity; Sign Similarity, which simplifies the calculation of similarity between tokens; and Dynamic Merge Rate, which enables image-adaptive token merging. Additionally, the hardware component that supports AdapTiV's algorithms, named the Adaptive Token Merging Engine, employs Sign-Driven Scheduling to conceal the overhead of token merging effectively. This engine integrates submodules such as a Sign Similarity Computing Unit, which calculates the similarity between tokens using a newly introduced similarity metric; a Sign Scratchpad, which is a lightweight, image-width-sized memory that stores previous tokens; a Sign Scratchpad Managing Unit, which controls the Sign Scratchpad; and a Token Integration Map to facilitate efficient, image-adaptive token merging. Our evaluations demonstrate that AdapTiV achieves, on average, 309.4 ×, 18.4×, 89.8×, 6.3× speedups and 262.1×, 21.5×, 496.6×, 11.2× improvements in energy efficiency over edge CPUs, edge GPUs, server CPUs, and server GPUs, while maintaining an accuracy loss below 1 % without additional training.
papers.abstract_zh: 视觉变压器（ViT）的出现通过利用自注意力机制在计算机视觉领域实现了新的性能飞跃。然而，ViT 的计算效率受到自注意力的二次复杂度和图像标记之间的冗余的限制。为了解决这些问题，人们探索了令牌合并策略，通过合并相似的令牌来减少输入大小。尽管如此，由于以下两个因素，实施令牌合并会导致延迟性能下降：低效计算和固定合并率性质。本文介绍了AdapTiV，这是一种新颖的软硬件协同设计的加速器，它通过图像自适应令牌合并来加速ViT，有效解决了上述挑战。在减少令牌合并开销并隐藏其在层归一化（LN）过程中的延迟的设计理念下，AdapTiV融合了本地匹配等算法创新，限制了令牌合并的搜索空间，从而降低了计算复杂度； Sign相似度，简化了token之间相似度的计算；动态合并率，可实现图像自适应令牌合并。此外，支持AdapTiV算法的硬件组件（称为自适应令牌合并引擎）采用符号驱动调度来有效隐藏令牌合并的开销。该引擎集成了符号相似度计算单元等子模块，该单元使用新引入的相似度度量来计算标记之间的相似度； Sign Scratchpad，这是一种轻量级、图像宽度大小的存储器，用于存储以前的令牌；标志便签本管理单元，其控制标志便签本；以及令牌集成图，以促进高效、图像自适应的令牌合并。我们的评估表明，与边缘 CPU、边缘 GPU、服务器 CPU 和服务器 GPU 相比，AdapTiV 平均实现了 309.4 ×、18.4 ×、89.8 ×、6.3 × 的加速和 262.1 ×、21.5 ×、496.6 ×、11.2 × 的能效改进，同时在无需额外训练的情况下将精度损失保持在 1% 以下。

papers.title: Ares-Flash: Efficient Parallel Integer Arithmetic Operations Using NAND Flash Memory.
papers.abstract: In-Flash Processing (IFP) has been proposed in recent years to realize computation ability inside NAND flash memory. Distinguished from processing-in-memory (PIM) and in-storage-processing (ISP), IFP reduces the data movement starting from the most bottom flash memory medium. It is especially beneficial to those applications that require high processing parallelism (e.g., huge databases, large-scale image processing, etc.). However, IFP works often require expensive extra hardware modification, which brings lots of energy dissipation and area overhead. Recent works, Parabit and Flash-Cosmos, try to implement calculations using flash memory with minimum hardware modification. But their accomplishments still remain at the level of simple bitwise operations, and thus it prevents their work from being widely adopted. In this work, we propose Ares-Flash, a new technology using flash memory to support more complex integer arithmetic operations (e.g., addition, accumulation, and multiplication). Ares leverages the designed page buffer to perform basic mechanisms: full-adder logic and bit-shift. Moreover, we construct computational operations using dedicated control sequences in the page buffer upon two basic mechanisms. Our experimental results indicate that Ares is highly efficient and significantly mitigates data movement from storage to memory or computing units (e.g., CPUs, GPUs). Quantitatively, Ares averagely improves performance and energy efficiency by 8.58×/9.89× and 97.9×/14× compared to the out-storage-processing(OSP)/instorage-processing(ISP) under accumulation tasks with real workloads. It also improves 8.2×/4.5× and 89×/13.2× when performing vector-vector multiplication on real-world workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 近年来，人们提出了闪存内处理（IFP）来实现NAND闪存内部的计算能力。与内存中处理 (PIM) 和存储中处理 (ISP) 不同，IFP 减少了从最底层闪存介质开始的数据移动。对于那些需要高处理并行性的应用程序（例如庞大的数据库、大规模图像处理等）尤其有利。然而，IFP工作往往需要昂贵的额外硬件修改，这带来了大量的能量耗散和面积开销。最近的作品Parabit和Flash-Cosmos尝试使用闪存以最小的硬件修改来实现计算。但他们的成就仍然停留在简单的按位运算的水平，因此阻碍了他们的工作被广泛采用。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Ares-Flash，这是一种使用闪存来支持更复杂的整数算术运算（例如加法、累加和乘法）的新技术。 Ares 利用设计的页缓冲区来执行基本机制：全加器逻辑和位移位。此外，我们在两种基本机制上使用页面缓冲区中的专用控制序列构建计算操作。我们的实验结果表明，Ares 非常高效，并且可以显着减少从存储到内存或计算单元（例如 CPU、GPU）的数据移动。从数量上看，在实际工作负载累积任务下，与外存储处理（OSP）/内存储处理（ISP）相比，Ares平均提高了8.58×/9.89×和97.9×/14×的性能和能效。在实际工作负载上执行向量-向量乘法时，它还提高了 8.2×/4.5× 和 89×/13.2×。

papers.title: Atomic Cache: Enabling Efficient Fine-Grained Synchronization with Relaxed Memory Consistency on GPGPUs Through In-Cache Atomic Operations.
papers.abstract: General-purpose graphics processing unit (GPGPU), widely recognized as an exceptional computing platform for de-ploying emerging parallel applications, requires strict adherence to atomicity and memory consistency models for shared variable synchronization. This is crucial to ensure deterministic execution and leverage the performance advantages of the GPGPU single-instruction -multiple-threads architecture. However, the escalating demand for shared variable updates across thread blocks, notably in applications like deep neural networks and graph analysis, significantly exacerbates the serialization overhead of atomic operations due to the von Neumann bottleneck. Additionally, the overhead introduced by memory fences supporting the memory consistency model further complicates this fine-grained synchronization requirement. To address these challenges, this paper proposes Atomic Cache, facilitating an In-Cache computing hardware-software co-design for GPGPUs. At the software level, we propose relaxed memory consistency based on non-ordering commutativity to alleviate the execution of in-cache atomic operations, thereby mitigating the performance overhead of memory fences. At the hardware level, we present the In-Situ Store Atomic Cache Macro, which empowers the Atomic Cache to efficiently execute atomic logic and arithmetic operations within the cache array. This innovation alleviates the von Neumann bottleneck associated with serialized execution of atomic operations. The experimental evaluation results demonstrate that the Atomic Cache can save more than 60% of memory access energy while incurring only 9.42% chip area overhead. Furthermore, it not only delivers an average speedup ratio of 2.59 × and an IPC performance improvement of 1.48× for RISC-V GPGPUs, but also achieves an average speedup ratio of 1.31 × and an IPC performance improvement of 39.92% when compared to state-of-the-art designs employing local atomic buffers.
papers.abstract_zh: 通用图形处理单元 (GPGPU) 被广泛认为是用于部署新兴并行应用程序的卓越计算平台，需要严格遵守共享变量同步的原子性和内存一致性模型。这对于确保确定性执行和利用 GPGPU 单指令多线程架构的性能优势至关重要。然而，跨线程块共享变量更新的需求不断增加，特别是在深度神经网络和图形分析等应用中，由于冯·诺依曼瓶颈，显着加剧了原子操作的序列化开销。此外，支持内存一致性模型的内存栅栏带来的开销进一步使这种细粒度同步要求变得复杂。为了应对这些挑战，本文提出了原子缓存，促进 GPGPU 的缓存内计算硬件-软件协同设计。在软件层面，我们提出基于无序交换性的宽松内存一致性，以减轻缓存内原子操作的执行，从而减轻内存栅栏的性能开销。在硬件层面，我们提出了原位存储原子缓存宏，它使原子缓存能够在缓存阵列内高效地执行原子逻辑和算术运算。这项创新缓解了与原子操作的串行执行相关的冯·诺依曼瓶颈。实验评估结果表明，Atomic Cache可以节省超过60%的内存访问能量，同时仅产生9.42%的芯片面积开销。此外，它不仅为 RISC-V GPGPU 提供了 2.59 倍的平均加速比和 1.48 倍的 IPC 性能提升，而且与采用本地原子缓冲区的最先进设计相比，还实现了 1.31 倍的平均加速比和 39.92% 的 IPC 性能提升。

papers.title: Azul: An Accelerator for Sparse Iterative Solvers Leveraging Distributed On-Chip Memory.
papers.abstract: Solving sparse systems of linear equations is a fundamental primitive in many numeric algorithms. Iterative solvers provide an efficient way of solving large, highly sparse systems. However, iterative solvers are inefficient on existing architectures because they perform computations with (1) poor short-term reuse, which causes frequent off-chip memory traffic; and (2) challenaing data dependences, which limit parallelism. We present Azul, a hardware accelerator that achieves high arithmetic intensity by keeping data in distributed on-chip SRAM. Azul is organized as a grid of tiles, each with a small memory and a simple processing element (PE). This enables keeping solver data on-chip across iterations, achieving high reuse. We present a novel scheduling algorithm that maps data and computation across PEs to avoid communication bottlenecks while achieving high parallelism, and a specialized PE that achieves high utilization of arithmetic units. When tested on a representative set of matrices for sparse iterative solvers, Azul is gmean 217 × faster than state-of-the art GPU implementations, 159× faster than a previously proposed accelerator for sparse iterative solvers, and 90 × faster than a previously proposed distributed-SRAM accelerator.
papers.abstract_zh: 求解稀疏线性方程组是许多数值算法的基本原语。迭代求解器提供了一种求解大型、高度稀疏系统的有效方法。然而，迭代求解器在现有架构上效率较低，因为它们执行计算时存在以下问题：(1) 短期重用性较差，导致频繁的片外内存流量； (2) 具有挑战性的数据依赖性，限制了并行性。我们推出了 Azul，这是一种硬件加速器，它通过将数据保存在分布式片上 SRAM 中来实现高算术强度。 Azul 被组织为一个图块网格，每个图块都有一个小内存和一个简单的处理元件 (PE)。这使得求解器数据能够在迭代过程中保留在片上，从而实现高重用。我们提出了一种新颖的调度算法，可以在 PE 之间映射数据和计算，以避免通信瓶颈，同时实现高并行性，以及实现算术单元高利用率的专用 PE。 When tested on a representative set of matrices for sparse iterative solvers, Azul is gmean 217 × faster than state-of-the art GPU implementations, 159× faster than a previously proposed accelerator for sparse iterative solvers, and 90 × faster than a previously proposed distributed-SRAM accelerator.

papers.title: BABOL: A Software-Defined NAND Flash Controller.
papers.abstract: NAND Flash Storage Controllers are a crucial component of Solid State Drives (SSDs). They provide an abstraction of Flash packages to the SSD firmware by translating high-level operations, such as a Page Program or a Block Erase, into lowlevel signals. In theory, the Open NAND Flash Interface (ONFI) specification standardizes this interface. In practice, however, every package supports optimized versions of the standard operations as well as non-standard operations. Writing a controller that exploits these optimizations is the only way to obtain competitive performance, but it makes for a highly intricate, error-prone, and non-portable controller development process. Compounding the issue is the fact that new generations of Flash packages are produced yearly, and non-standard optimization techniques are often presented in the literature. Modifying rigid hardware controllers to support these advancements is extremely challenging, making it difficult to rapidly prototype new SSDs and exploit the full potential of Flash memory. To address this, we propose Babol, a software-defined Flash controller architecture that provides generic hardware building blocks that can be flexibly combined via software to express complex, package-optimized Flash operations. We implemented two flavors of BABOL in an FPGA setting and experimented with several commercial off-the-shelf Flash packages. Our results show that the flexibility that Babol brings far outweighs the marginal amounts of performance and area it requires. We open source our controller, including its unique software programming environment, which we believe can make SSD controller development more productive for seasoned SSD Architects and make prototyping accessible for newcomers who want to join the field.
