Finally, we observe that carefully-selected modular systems achieve fidelity improvements on a range of benchmark circuits.
papers.abstract_zh: 固定频率传输量子计算机 (QC) 在相干时间、可寻址性和门保真度方面取得了进步。不幸的是，这些设备受到片上量子位数量的限制，限制了处理能力并减缓了容错的进展。尽管新兴的 transmon 设备具有超过 100 个量子位，但构建足够大的 QC 来有意义地展示量子优势需要克服许多设计挑战。例如，由于制造精度有限，当今的传输量子比特存在显着变化。因此，除非当前制造技术得到显着改进，否则通过构建具有更多量子位的更大的单个芯片来扩展质量控制会受到设备变化的阻碍。降低 QC 性能的严重设备变异称为缺陷。在这里，我们重点关注一种称为频率冲突的特定缺陷。当传输频率发生冲突时，它们的差异会落在限制两个量子位门保真度的范围内。在较大的 QC 上发生频率冲突的可能性更大，导致无冲突产量随着片上量子位数量的增加而下降。作为解决方案，我们建议通过将量子小芯片集成到量子多芯片模块 (MCM) 中来利用与较小 QC 相关的更高产量。产量、门性能和基于应用的分析显示了通过模块化进行 QC 扩展的可行性。我们的结果表明，相对于单片设计，小芯片架构受益于 ≲5 量子位机器的 9.6 – 92.6 × 平均良率改进。此外，我们的模拟探索了小芯片系统的设计空间，并发现了能够证明平均双量子位门不保真度减少最多为单片系统的 0.815 倍的配置。最后，我们观察到精心挑选的模块化系统在一系列基准电路上实现了保真度改进。

papers.title: Self-Reinforcing Memoization for Cryptography Calculations in Secure Memory Systems.
papers.abstract: Modern memory systems use encryption and message authentication codes to ensure confidentiality and integrity. Encryption and integrity verification rely on cryptography calculations, which are slow. To hide the latency of cryptography calculations, prior works exploit the fact that many cryptography steps only require a memory block’s write counter (i.e., a value that increases whenever the block is written to memory), but not the block itself. As such, memory controller (MC) caches counters so that MC can start calculating before missing blocks arrive from memory.Irregular workloads suffer from high counter miss rates, however, just like they suffer from high miss rates of page table entries. Many prior works have looked at the problem of page table entry misses for irregular workloads, but not the problem of counter misses for the irregular workloads.This paper addresses the memory latency overheads that irregular workloads suffer due to their high counter miss rate. We observe many (e.g., unlimited number of) counters can have the same value. As such, we propose memoizing cryptography calculations for hot counter values. When a counter arrives from memory, MC can use the counter value to look up a memoization table to quickly obtain the counter’s memoized results instead of slowly recalculating them. To maximize memoization table hit rate, we observe whenever writing a block to memory, increasing its counter to any value higher than the current counter value can satisfy the security requirement of always using different counter values to encrypt the same block. As such, we also propose a memoization-aware counter update: when writing a block to memory, increase its counter to a value whose cryptography calculation is currently memoized. We refer to memoizing the calculation results of counters and the corresponding memoization-aware counter update collectively as Self-Reinforcing Memoization for Cryptography Calculations (RMCC). Our evaluations show that RMCC improves average performance by 6% compared to the state-of-the-art. On average across the lifetimes of different workloads, RMCC accelerates decryption and verification for 92% of counter misses.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代存储系统使用加密和消息验证代码来确保机密性和完整性。加密和完整性验证依赖于密码学计算，速度较慢。为了隐藏密码学计算的延迟，先前的工作利用了这样一个事实：许多密码学步骤只需要内存块的写入计数器（即，每当块写入内存时就会增加的值），而不需要块本身。因此，内存控制器 (MC) 会缓存计数器，以便 MC 可以在丢失的块从内存到达之前开始计算。然而，不规则的工作负载会遭受高计数器丢失率的影响，就像它们会遭受页表条目高丢失率的影响一样。许多先前的工作已经研究了不规则工作负载的页表条目缺失问题，但没有关注不规则工作负载的计数器缺失问题。本文解决了不规则工作负载由于其高计数器缺失率而遭受的内存延迟开销。我们观察到许多（例如，无限数量）计数器可以具有相同的值。因此，我们建议记忆热计数器值的密码学计算。当计数器从内存到达时，MC可以使用计数器值查找记忆表来快速获取计数器的记忆结果，而不是慢慢地重新计算它们。为了最大化记忆表命中率，我们观察到每当将块写入内存时，将其计数器增加到高于当前计数器值的任何值都可以满足始终使用不同计数器值来加密同一块的安全要求。因此，我们还提出了一种记忆感知计数器更新：将块写入内存时，将其计数器增加到当前已记忆密码计算的值。我们将记忆计数器的计算结果和相应的记忆感知计数器更新统称为密码学计算的自我强化记忆（RMCC）。我们的评估表明，与最先进的技术相比，RMCC 将平均性能提高了 6%。平均而言，在不同工作负载的生命周期中，RMCC 可以加速 92% 的计数器未命中的解密和验证。

papers.title: SIMR: Single Instruction Multiple Request Processing for Energy-Efficient Data Center Microservices.
papers.abstract: Contemporary data center servers process thousands of similar, independent requests per minute. In the interest of programmer productivity and ease of scaling, workloads in data centers have shifted from single monolithic processes toward a micro and nanoservice software architecture. As a result, single servers are now packed with many threads executing the same, relatively small task on different data.State-of-the-art data centers run these microservices on multi-core CPUs. However, the flexibility offered by traditional CPUs comes at an energy-efficiency cost. The Multiple Instruction Multiple Data execution model misses opportunities to aggregate the similarity in contemporary microservices. We observe that the Single Instruction Multiple Thread execution model, employed by GPUs, provides better thread scaling and has the potential to reduce frontend and memory system energy consumption. However, contemporary GPUs are ill-suited for the latency-sensitive microservice space.To exploit the similarity in contemporary microservices, while maintaining acceptable latency, we propose the Request Processing Unit (RPU). The RPU combines elements of out-of-order CPUs with lockstep thread aggregation mechanisms found in GPUs to execute microservices in a Single Instruction Multiple Request (SIMR) fashion. To complement the RPU, we also propose a SIMR-aware software stack that uses novel mechanisms to batch requests based on their predicted control-flow, split batches based on predicted latency divergence and map per-request memory allocations to maximize coalescing opportunities. Our resulting RPU system processes 5. 7 × more requests/joule than multi-core CPUs, while increasing single thread latency by only 1. ×.
papers.abstract_zh: Contemporary data center servers process thousands of similar, independent requests per minute.为了提高程序员的工作效率和易于扩展，数据中心的工作负载已从单一的整体流程转向微服务和纳米服务软件架构。因此，单个服务器现在挤满了许多线程，在不同的数据上执行相同的、相对较小的任务。最先进的数据中心在多核 CPU 上运行这些微服务。 However, the flexibility offered by traditional CPUs comes at an energy-efficiency cost. The Multiple Instruction Multiple Data execution model misses opportunities to aggregate the similarity in contemporary microservices.我们观察到 GPU 采用的单指令多线程执行模型提供了更好的线程扩展，并且有可能降低前端和内存系统的能耗。然而，当代 GPU 不适合延迟敏感的微服务空间。为了利用当代微服务的相似性，同时保持可接受的延迟，我们提出了请求处理单元（RPU）。 RPU 将无序 CPU 的元素与 GPU 中的锁步线程聚合机制相结合，以单指令多请求 (SIMR) 方式执行微服务。为了补充 RPU，我们还提出了一个 SIMR 感知软件堆栈，该堆栈使用新颖的机制根据预测的控制流对请求进行批处理，根据预测的延迟差异进行分批，并映射每个请求的内存分配以最大化合并机会。 Our resulting RPU system processes 5. 7 × more requests/joule than multi-core CPUs, while increasing single thread latency by only 1. ×.

papers.title: Skipper: Enabling efficient SNN training through activation-checkpointing and time-skipping.
papers.abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) are a highly efficient signal processing mechanism in biological systems that have inspired a plethora of research efforts aimed at translating their energy efficiency to computational platforms. Efficient training approaches are critical for the successful deployment of SNNs. Compared to mainstream deep neural networks (ANNs), training SNNs is far more challenging due to complex neural dynamics that evolve with time and their discrete, binary computing paradigm. Back-propagation-through-time (BPTT) with surrogate gradients has recently emerged as an effective technique to train deep SNNs directly. SNN-BPTT, however, has a major drawback in that it has a high memory requirement that increases with the number of timesteps. SNNs generally result from the discretization of Ordinary Differential Equations, due to which the sequence length must be typically longer than RNNs, compounding the time dependence problem. It, therefore, becomes hard to train deep SNNs on a single or multi-GPU setup with sufficiently large batch sizes or timesteps, and extended periods of training are required to achieve reasonable network performance. In this work, we reduce the memory requirements of BPTT in SNNs to enable the training of deeper SNNs with more timesteps (T). For this, we leverage the notion of activation re-computation in the context of SNN training that enables the GPU memory to scale sub-linearly with increasing time-steps. We observe that naively deploying the re-computation based approach leads to a considerable computational overhead. To solve this, we propose a time-skipped BPTT approximation technique, called Skipper, for SNNs, that not only alleviates this computation overhead, but also lowers memory consumption further with little to no loss of accuracy. We show the efficacy of our proposed technique by comparing it against a popular method for memory footprint reduction during training. Our evaluations on 5 state-of-the-art networks and 4 datasets show that for a constant batch size and time-steps, skipper reduces memory usage by 3.3× to 8.4× (6.7× on average) over baseline SNN-BPTT. It also achieves a speedup of 29% to 70% over the checkpointed approach and of 4% to 40% over the baseline approach. For a constant memory budget, skipper can scale to an order of magnitude higher timesteps compared to baseline SNN-BPTT.
papers.abstract_zh: 尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 是生物系统中一种高效的信号处理机制，激发了大量旨在将其能源效率转化为计算平台的研究工作。有效的训练方法对于 SNN 的成功部署至关重要。与主流深度神经网络 (ANN) 相比，由于随时间演变的复杂神经动力学及其离散的二进制计算范式，训练 SNN 更具挑战性。具有代理梯度的时间反向传播 (BPTT) 最近已成为直接训练深度 SNN 的有效技术。然而，SNN-BPTT 有一个主要缺点，即它的内存需求随着时间步数的增加而增加。 SNN 通常由常微分方程的离散化产生，因此序列长度通常必须比 RNN 长，从而加剧了时间依赖性问题。因此，在具有足够大的批量大小或时间步长的单或多 GPU 设置上训练深度 SNN 变得很困难，并且需要延长训练时间才能实现合理的网络性能。在这项工作中，我们减少了 SNN 中 BPTT 的内存需求，以便能够使用更多时间步 (T) 训练更深的 SNN。为此，我们在 SNN 训练的背景下利用激活重新计算的概念，使 GPU 内存能够随着时间步长的增加而实现亚线性扩展。我们观察到，天真地部署基于重新计算的方法会导致相当大的计算开销。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种用于 SNN 的时间跳跃 BPTT 近似技术，称为 Skipper，它不仅减轻了这种计算开销，而且还进一步降低了内存消耗，而几乎没有损失精度。我们通过将其与训练期间减少内存占用的流行方法进行比较来展示我们提出的技术的有效性。我们对 5 个最先进的网络和 4 个数据集的评估表明，对于恒定的批量大小和时间步长，Skipper 比基线 SNN-BPTT 减少了 3.3 倍到 8.4 倍（平均 6.7 倍）的内存使用量。与检查点方法相比，它的速度提高了 29% 到 70%，与基线方法相比，速度提高了 4% 到 40%。对于恒定的内存预算，与基线 SNN-BPTT 相比，Skipper 可以扩展到高一个数量级的时间步长。

papers.title: Sparse Attention Acceleration with Synergistic In-Memory Pruning and On-Chip Recomputation.
papers.abstract: As its core computation, a self-attention mechanism gauges pairwise correlations across the entire input sequence. Despite favorable performance, calculating pairwise correlations is prohibitively costly. While recent work has shown the benefits of runtime pruning of elements with low attention scores, the quadratic complexity of self-attention mechanisms and their on-chip memory capacity demands are overlooked. This work addresses these constraints by architecting an accelerator, called SPRINT 1 , which leverages the inherent parallelism of ReRAM crossbar arrays to compute attention scores in an approximate manner. Our design prunes the low attention scores using a lightweight analog thresholding circuitry within ReRAM, enabling SPRINT to fetch only a small subset of relevant data to on-chip memory. To mitigate potential negative repercussions for model accuracy, SPRINT re-computes the attention scores for the few fetched data in digital. The combined in-memory pruning and on-chip recompute of the relevant attention scores enables SPRINT to transform quadratic complexity to a merely linear one. In addition, we identify and leverage a dynamic spatial locality between the adjacent attention operations even after pruning, which eliminates costly yet redundant data fetches. We evaluate our proposed technique on a wide range of state-of-the-art transformer models. On average, SPRINT yields 7.5× speedup and 19.6× energy reduction when total l6KB on-chip memory is used, while virtually on par with iso-accuracy of the baseline models (on average 0.36% degradation).
papers.abstract_zh: 作为其核心计算，自注意力机制衡量整个输入序列的成对相关性。尽管性能良好，但计算成对相关性的成本却非常高。虽然最近的工作表明了对注意力分数较低的元素进行运行时修剪的好处，但自注意力机制的二次复杂性及其片上内存容量需求却被忽视了。这项工作通过构建一个名为 SPRINT 1 的加速器来解决这些限制，该加速器利用 ReRAM 交叉阵列固有的并行性以近似方式计算注意力分数。我们的设计使用 ReRAM 中的轻量级模拟阈值电路来修剪低注意力分数，使 SPRINT 能够仅将一小部分相关数据提取到片上存储器。为了减轻对模型准确性的潜在负面影响，SPRINT 重新计算了少数获取的数字数据的注意力分数。内存中剪枝和相关注意力分数的片上重新计算相结合，使 SPRINT 能够将二次复杂度转变为线性复杂度。此外，即使在修剪之后，我们也能识别并利用相邻注意力操作之间的动态空间局部性，这消除了昂贵但冗余的数据获取。我们在各种最先进的变压器模型上评估我们提出的技术。平均而言，当使用总 16KB 片上内存时，SPRINT 可以实现 7.5 倍的加速和 19.6 倍的能耗降低，同时几乎与基线模型的等精度相当（平均下降 0.36%）。

papers.title: Sparseloop: An Analytical Approach To Sparse Tensor Accelerator Modeling.
papers.abstract: In recent years, many accelerators have been proposed to efficiently process sparse tensor algebra applications (e.g., sparse neural networks). However, these proposals are single points in a large and diverse design space. The lack of systematic description and modeling support for these sparse tensor accelerators impedes hardware designers from efficient and effective design space exploration. This paper first presents a unified taxonomy to systematically describe the diverse sparse tensor accelerator design space. Based on the proposed taxonomy, it then introduces Sparseloop, the first fast, accurate, and flexible analytical modeling framework to enable early-stage evaluation and exploration of sparse tensor accelerators. Sparseloop comprehends a large set of architecture specifications, including various dataflows and sparse acceleration features (e.g., elimination of zero-based compute). Using these specifications, Sparseloop evaluates a design’s processing speed and energy efficiency while accounting for data movement and compute incurred by the employed dataflow, including the savings and overhead introduced by the sparse acceleration features using stochastic density models. Across representative accelerator designs and workloads, Sparseloop achieves over 2000× faster modeling speed than cycle-level simulations, maintains relative performance trends, and achieves 0.1% to 8% average error. The paper also presents example use cases of Sparseloop in different accelerator design flows to reveal important design insights.
papers.abstract_zh: 近年来，人们提出了许多加速器来有效处理稀疏张量代数应用（例如稀疏神经网络）。然而，这些建议只是庞大而多样化的设计空间中的单点。这些稀疏张量加速器缺乏系统描述和建模支持，阻碍了硬件设计人员高效且有效的设计空间探索。本文首先提出了一个统一的分类法来系统地描述多样化的稀疏张量加速器设计空间。然后，基于所提出的分类法，介绍了 Sparseloop，这是第一个快速、准确且灵活的分析建模框架，可实现稀疏张量加速器的早期评估和探索。 Sparseloop 包含大量架构规范，包括各种数据流和稀疏加速功能（例如，消除从零开始的计算）。使用这些规范，Sparseloop 可以评估设计的处理速度和能源效率，同时考虑所使用的数据流所产生的数据移动和计算，包括使用随机密度模型的稀疏加速功能带来的节省和开销。在代表性的加速器设计和工作负载中，Sparseloop 的建模速度比周期级仿真快 2000 倍以上，保持相对性能趋势，并实现 0.1% 至 8% 的平均误差。本文还介绍了 Sparseloop 在不同加速器设计流程中的示例用例，以揭示重要的设计见解。

papers.title: Speculative Code Compaction: Eliminating Dead Code via Speculative Microcode Transformations.
papers.abstract: The computing landscape has been increasingly characterized by processor architectures with increasing core counts, while a majority of the software applications remain inherently sequential. Although state-of-the-art compilers feature sophisticated optimizations, a significant chunk of wasteful computation persists due to the presence of data-dependent operations and irregular control-flow patterns that are unpredictable at compile-time. This work presents speculative code compaction (SCC), a novel microarchitectural technique that significantly enhances the capabilities of the microcode engine to aggressively and speculatively eliminate dead code from hot code regions resident in the micro-op cache, and further generate a compact stream of micro-ops, based on dynamically predicted machine code invariants. SCC also extends existing micro-op cache designs to co-host multiple versions of unoptimized and speculatively optimized micro-op sequences, providing the fetch engine with significant flexibility to dynamically choose from and stream the appropriate set of micro-ops, as and when deemed profitable.SCC is a minimally-invasive technique that can be implemented at the processor front-end using a simple ALU and a register context table, and is yet able to substantially accelerate the performance of already compile-time optimized and machine-tuned code by an average of 6% (and as much as 30%), with an average of 12% (and as much as 24%) savings in energy consumption, while eliminating the need for profiling and offering increased adaptability to changing datasets and workload patterns.
papers.abstract_zh: 计算环境越来越以处理器架构为特征，核心数量不断增加，而大多数软件应用程序本质上仍然是顺序的。尽管最先进的编译器具有复杂的优化功能，但由于存在依赖于数据的操作和编译时不可预测的不规则控制流模式，大量浪费的计算仍然存在。这项工作提出了推测性代码压缩（SCC），这是一种新颖的微架构技术，它显着增强了微代码引擎的能力，可以主动地、推测性地消除驻留在微操作缓存中的热代码区域中的死代码，并基于动态预测的机器代码不变量进一步生成紧凑的微操作流。 SCC 还扩展了现有的微操作缓存设计，以共同托管未优化和推测优化的微操作序列的多个版本，为获取引擎提供了显着的灵活性，以便在认为有利可图时动态选择和流式传输适当的微操作集。SCC 是一种微创技术，可以使用简单的 ALU 和寄存器上下文表在处理器前端实现，并且能够将已编译时优化和机器调整的代码的性能平均大幅提高6%（最高可达 30%），平均能耗节省 12%（最高可达 24%），同时消除了分析需求，并增强了对不断变化的数据集和工作负载模式的适应性。

papers.title: SwiftDir: Secure Cache Coherence without Overprotection.
papers.abstract: Cache coherence states have recently been exploited to leak secrets through timing-channel attacks. The root cause lies in the fact that shared data in state Exclusive (E) and state Shared (S) are served from different cache layers. The state-of-the-art countermeasure—S-MESI—serves both E- and S-state shared data from the last-level cache (LLC) by explicitly synchronizing the Modified (M) state across private caches and the LLC. This has to sacrifice the silent upgrade feature that MESI introduces for speedup. Moreover, it enforces protection to not only exploitable shared data but also unshared data. This further slows down performance, especially for write-after-read intensive applications. In this paper, we propose SwiftDir to efficiently secure cache coherence against cover-channel attacks without overprotection. SwiftDir fundamentally narrows down the protection scope to write-protected data. Such exploitable shared data can be uniquely identified with the write-protection permission in the memory management unit (MMU) and do not necessarily transit to state M. We validate this idea through tracing system calls of shared libraries on Linux. We then investigate all three commercial cache architectures (i.e., PIPT, VIPT, and VIVT) and find it feasible to hitchhike the address translation process to transmit the write-protection information from the MMU to the coherence controller. Then SwiftDir enforces protection over only write-protected data by serving all requests toward them directly from the LLC with a constant latency. This not only simplifies how MESI handles write-protected data but also avoids how S-MESI overprotects them. Meanwhile, SwiftDir still preserves silent upgrade for efficient handling of unshared data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our SwiftDir can secure cache coherence while outperforming not only secure SMESI but also unprotected MESI.
papers.abstract_zh: 最近，缓存一致性状态被用来通过定时通道攻击来泄露秘密。根本原因在于，Exclusive (E) 状态和 Shared (S) 状态下的共享数据是由不同的缓存层提供的。最先进的对策 - S-MESI - 通过在私有缓存和 LLC 之间显式同步修改 (M) 状态，从最后一级缓存 (LLC) 提供 E 状态和 S 状态共享数据。这就必须牺牲 MESI 为加速而引入的静默升级功能。此外，它不仅对可利用的共享数据实施保护，还对非共享数据实施保护。这进一步降低了性能，特别是对于读后写密集型应用程序。在本文中，我们提出 SwiftDir 来有效地保护缓存一致性，防止覆盖通道攻击，而不会过度保护。 SwiftDir 从根本上将保护范围缩小到写保护数据。这种可利用的共享数据可以通过内存管理单元（MMU）中的写保护权限来唯一标识，并且不一定会转变为状态M。我们通过跟踪Linux上共享库的系统调用来验证这个想法。然后，我们研究了所有三种商业缓存架构（即 PIPT、VIPT 和 VIVT），并发现搭上地址转换过程的便车将写保护信息从 MMU 传输到一致性控制器是可行的。然后，SwiftDir 通过以恒定的延迟直接从 LLC 向数据提供所有请求，从而仅对写保护数据实施保护。这不仅简化了 MESI 处理写保护数据的方式，而且还避免了 S-MESI 对它们的过度保护。同时，SwiftDir 仍然保留静默升级，以高效处理非共享数据。大量实验表明，我们的 SwiftDir 可以确保缓存一致性，同时不仅优于安全的 SMESI，还优于未受保护的 MESI。

papers.title: Towards Developing High Performance RISC-V Processors Using Agile Methodology.
papers.abstract: While research has shown that the agile chip design methodology is promising to sustain the scaling of computing performance in a more efficient way, it is still of limited usage in actual applications due to two major obstacles: 1) Lack of tool-chain and developing framework supporting agile chip design, especially for large-scale modern processors. 2) The conventional verification methods are less agile and become a major bottleneck of the entire process. To tackle both issues, we propose MINJIE, an open-source platform supporting agile processor development flow. MINJIE integrates a broad set of tools for logic design, functional verification, performance modelling, pre-silicon validation and debugging for better development efficiency of state-of-the-art processor designs. We demonstrate the usage and effectiveness of MINJIE by building two generations of an open-source superscalar out-of-order RISC-V processor code-named XIANGSHAN using agile methodologies. We quantify the performance of XIANGSHAN using SPEC CPU2006 benchmarks and demonstrate that XIANGSHAN achieves industry-competitive performance.
papers.abstract_zh: 虽然研究表明敏捷芯片设计方法有望以更有效的方式维持计算性能的扩展，但由于两个主要障碍，它在实际应用中的使用仍然有限：1）缺乏支持敏捷芯片设计的工具链和开发框架，特别是对于大规模现代处理器。 2）传统的验证方法敏捷性较差，成为整个流程的主要瓶颈。为了解决这两个问题，我们提出了 MINJIE，一个支持敏捷处理器开发流程的开源平台。 MINJIE 集成了一系列用于逻辑设计、功能验证、性能建模、硅前验证和调试的工具，以提高最先进处理器设计的开发效率。我们通过使用敏捷方法构建两代开源超标量乱序 RISC-V 处理器（代号 XIANGSHAN）来展示 MINJIE 的用法和有效性。我们使用 SPEC CPU2006 基准测试来量化象山的性能，并证明象山实现了具有行业竞争力的性能。

papers.title: Translation-optimized Memory Compression for Capacity.
papers.abstract: The demand for memory is ever increasing. Many prior works have explored hardware memory compression to increase effective memory capacity. However, prior works compress and pack/migrate data at a small - memory block-level - granularity; this introduces an additional block-level translation after the page-level virtual address translation. In general, the smaller the granularity of address translation, the higher the translation overhead. As such, this additional block-level translation exacerbates the well-known address translation problem for large and/or irregular workloads. A promising solution is to only save memory from cold (i.e., less recently accessed) pages without saving memory from hot (i.e., more recently accessed) pages (e.g., keep the hot pages uncompressed); this avoids block-level translation overhead for hot pages. However, it still faces two challenges. First, after a compressed cold page becomes hot again, migrating the page to a full 4KB DRAM location still adds another level (albeit page-level, instead of block-level) of translation on top of existing virtual address translation. Second, only compressing cold data require compressing them very aggressively to achieve high overall memory savings; decompressing very aggressively compressed data is very slow (e.g., $\gt 800 ns$ assuming the latest Deflate ASIC in industry). This paper presents Translation-optimized Memory Compression for Capacity (TMCC) to tackle the two challenges above. To address the first challenge, we propose compressing page table blocks in hardware to opportunistically embed compression translations into them in a software-transparent manner to effectively prefetch compression translations during a page walk, instead of serially fetching them after the walk. To address the second challenge, we perform a large design space exploration across many hardware configurations and diverse workloads to derive and implement in HDL an ASIC Deflate that is specialized for memory; for memory pages, it is 4X as fast as the state-of-the art ASIC Deflate, with little to no sacrifice in compression ratio. Our evaluations show that for large and/or irregular workloads, TMCC can either improve performance by 14% without sacrificing effective capacity or provide 2.2x the effective capacity without sacrificing performance compared to a state-of-the-art hardware memory compression for capacity.
papers.abstract_zh: 对内存的需求不断增加。许多先前的工作已经探索了硬件内存压缩以增加有效内存容量。然而，先前的工作以较小的内存块级粒度压缩和打包/迁移数据；这在页级虚拟地址转换之后引入了额外的块级转换。一般来说，地址转换的粒度越小，转换开销就越高。因此，这种额外的块级转换加剧了大型和/或不规则工作负载的众所周知的地址转换问题。一个有前途的解决方案是仅从冷（即最近访问较少）页面节省内存，而不从热（即最近访问较多）页面节省内存（例如，保持热页未压缩）；这避免了热页的块级转换开销。然而，它仍然面临两个挑战。首先，在压缩的冷页再次变热后，将该页迁移到完整的 4KB DRAM 位置仍然会在现有虚拟地址转换之上添加另一个级别的转换（尽管是页级，而不是块级）。其次，仅压缩冷数据就需要非常积极地压缩它们，以实现较高的总体内存节省；解压缩非常激进的压缩数据非常慢（例如，假设业界最新的 Deflate ASIC，$\gt 800 ns$）。本文提出了翻译优化的容量内存压缩（TMCC）来解决上述两个挑战。为了解决第一个挑战，我们建议在硬件中压缩页表块，以软件透明的方式机会性地将压缩翻译嵌入到其中，从而在页面遍历期间有效地预取压缩翻译，而不是在遍历之后串行获取它们。为了解决第二个挑战，我们在许多硬件配置和不同的工作负载中进行了大型设计空间探索，以在 HDL 中派生并实现专门用于内存的 ASIC Deflate；对于内存页面，它的速度是最先进的 ASIC Deflate 的 4 倍，并且几乎不牺牲压缩比。我们的评估表明，对于大型和/或不规则工作负载，与最先进的硬件内存压缩容量相比，TMCC 可以在不牺牲有效容量的情况下将性能提高 14%，或者在不牺牲性能的情况下提供 2.2 倍的有效容量。

papers.title: Treebeard: An Optimizing Compiler for Decision Tree Based ML Inference.
papers.abstract: Decision tree ensembles are among the most commonly used machine learning models. These models are used in a wide range of applications and are deployed at scale. Decision tree ensemble inference is usually performed with libraries such as XGBoost, LightGBM, and Sklearn. These libraries incorporate a fixed set of optimizations for the hardware targets they support. However, maintaining these optimizations is prohibitively expensive with the evolution of hardware. Further, they do not specialize the inference code to the model being used, leaving significant performance on the table. This paper presents TREEBEARD, an optimizing compiler that progressively lowers the inference computation to optimized CPU code through multiple intermediate abstractions. By applying model-specific optimizations at the higher levels, tree walk optimizations at the middle level, and machine-specific optimizations lower down, TREEBEARD can specialize inference code for each model on each supported CPU target. TREEBEARD combines several novel optimizations at various abstraction levels to mitigate architectural bottlenecks and enable SIMD vectorization of tree walks. We implement TREEBEARD using the MLIR compiler infrastructure and demonstrate its utility by evaluating it on a diverse set of benchmarks. TREEBEARD is significantly faster than state-of-the-art systems, XGBoost, Treelite and Hummingbird, by 2.6×, 4.7× and 5.4× respectively in a single-core execution setting, and by 2.3×, 2.7× and 14× respectively in multi-core settings.
papers.abstract_zh: 决策树集成是最常用的机器学习模型之一。这些模型用于广泛的应用程序并大规模部署。决策树集成推理通常使用 XGBoost、LightGBM 和 Sklearn 等库进行。这些库针对它们支持的硬件目标包含一组固定的优化。然而，随着硬件的发展，维持这些优化的成本极其昂贵。此外，他们没有将推理代码专门用于所使用的模型，从而导致性能显着下降。本文介绍了 TREEBEARD，这是一种优化编译器，可通过多个中间抽象逐步将推理计算降低到优化的 CPU 代码。通过在较高级别应用特定于模型的优化、在中间级别应用树遍历优化以及在较低级别应用特定于机器的优化，TREEBEARD 可以在每个受支持的 CPU 目标上为每个模型专门化推理代码。 TREEBEARD 结合了多种抽象级别的新颖优化，以缓解架构瓶颈并实现树遍历的 SIMD 矢量化。我们使用 MLIR 编译器基础设施实现 TREEBEARD，并通过在不同的基准测试集上对其进行评估来展示其实用性。 TREEBEARD 的速度明显快于最先进的系统 XGBoost、Treelite 和 Hummingbird，在单核执行设置中分别快 2.6 倍、4.7 倍和 5.4 倍，在多核设置中分别快 2.3 倍、2.7 倍和 14 倍。

papers.title: Vulkan-Sim: A GPU Architecture Simulator for Ray Tracing.
papers.abstract: Ray tracing can generate photorealistic images with more convincing visual effects compared to rasterization. Recent hardware advances have enabled ray tracing to be applied in real-time. Current GPUs feature a dedicated ray tracing acceleration unit, and game developers have started to make use of ray tracing APIs to bring more realistic graphics to their players. Industry cooperatively contributed to Vulkan, which recently introduced an open-standard API for ray tracing. However, little has been disclosed about the mapping of this API to hardware. In this paper, we introduce Vulkan-Sim, a detailed cycle-level simulator for enabling architecture research for ray tracing. We extend GPGPU-Sim, integrating it with Mesa, an open-source graphics library to support the Vulkan API, and add dedicated ray traversal and intersection units. We also demonstrate an explicit mapping of the Vulkan ray tracing pipeline to a modern GPU using a technique we call delayed intersection and any-hit execution. Additionally we evaluate several ray tracing workloads with Vulkan-Sim, identifying bottlenecks and inefficiencies of the ray tracing hardware we model. To demonstrate the utility of Vulkan-Sim we conduct two case studies evaluating techniques recently proposed or deployed by industry targeting enhanced ray tracing performance.
papers.abstract_zh: Ray tracing can generate photorealistic images with more convincing visual effects compared to rasterization. Recent hardware advances have enabled ray tracing to be applied in real-time.当前的 GPU 具有专用的光线追踪加速单元，游戏开发人员已开始利用光线追踪 API 为玩家带来更真实的图形。 Industry cooperatively contributed to Vulkan, which recently introduced an open-standard API for ray tracing. However, little has been disclosed about the mapping of this API to hardware. In this paper, we introduce Vulkan-Sim, a detailed cycle-level simulator for enabling architecture research for ray tracing.我们扩展了 GPGPU-Sim，将其与开源图形库 Mesa 集成以支持 Vulkan API，并添加专用的光线遍历和交叉单元。我们还使用我们称为延迟交集和任意命中执行的技术演示了 Vulkan 光线追踪管道到现代 GPU 的显式映射。此外，我们使用 Vulkan-Sim 评估多个光线追踪工作负载，识别我们建模的光线追踪硬件的瓶颈和低效率。为了展示 Vulkan-Sim 的实用性，我们进行了两个案例研究，评估行业最近提出或部署的旨在增强光线追踪性能的技术。

papers.title: Whisper: Profile-Guided Branch Misprediction Elimination for Data Center Applications.
papers.abstract: Modern data center applications experience frequent branch mispredictions– degrading performance, increasing cost, and reducing energy efficiency in data centers. Even the state-of the-art branch predictor, TAGE-SC-L, suffers from an average branch Mispredictions Per Kilo Instructions (branch-MPKI) of 3.0 (0.5-7.2) for these applications since their large code footprints exhaust TAGE-SC-L’s intended capacity. In this work, we propose Whisper, a novel profile-guided mechanism to avoid branch mispredictions. Whisper investigates the in-production profile of data center applications to identify precise program contexts that lead to branch mispredictions. Corresponding prediction hints are then inserted into code to strategically avoid those mispredictions during program execution. Whisper presents three novel profile-guided techniques: (1) hashed history correlation which efficiently encodes hard-to-predict correlations in branch history using lightweight Boolean formulas, (2) randomized formula testing which selects a locally-optimal Boolean formula from a randomly selected subset of possible formulas to predict a branch, and (3) the extension of Read-Once Monotone Boolean Formulas with Implication and Converse Non-Implication to improve the branch history coverage of these formulas with minimal overhead. We evaluate Whisper on 12 widely-used data center applications and demonstrate that Whisper enables traditional branch predictors to achieve a speedup close to that of an ideal branch predictor. Specifically, Whisper achieves an average speedup of 2.8% (0.4%-4.6%) by reducing 16.8% (1.7%-32.4%) of branch mispredictions over TAGE-SC-L and outperforms the state-of the-art profile-guided branch prediction mechanisms by 7.9% on average.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代数据中心应用程序经常遇到分支错误预测，从而降低数据中心的性能、增加成本并降低能源效率。即使是最先进的分支预测器 TAGE-SC-L，对于这些应用程序来说，每公斤指令的平均分支错误预测 (branch-MPKI) 也为 3.0 (0.5-7.2)，因为它们的代码占用量很大，耗尽了 TAGE-SC-L 的预期容量。 In this work, we propose Whisper, a novel profile-guided mechanism to avoid branch mispredictions. Whisper 调查数据中心应用程序的生产概况，以识别导致分支错误预测的精确程序上下文。 Corresponding prediction hints are then inserted into code to strategically avoid those mispredictions during program execution. Whisper 提出了三种新颖的配置引导技术：(1) 哈希历史相关性，使用轻量级布尔公式有效地编码分支历史中难以预测的相关性；(2) 随机公式测试，从随机选择的可能公式子集中选择局部最优布尔公式来预测分支；(3) 扩展具有蕴涵和逆非蕴涵的一次性读取单调布尔公式，以最小的开销提高这些公式的分支历史覆盖率。我们在 12 个广泛使用的数据中心应用程序上评估 Whisper，并证明 Whisper 使传统分支预测器能够实现接近理想分支预测器的加速。具体来说，Whisper 比 TAGE-SC-L 减少了 16.8% (1.7%-32.4%) 的分支错误预测，平均加速了 2.8% (0.4%-4.6%)，并且比最先进的配置文件引导分支预测机制平均提高了 7.9%。

papers.title: XPGraph: XPline-Friendly Persistent Memory Graph Stores for Large-Scale Evolving Graphs.
papers.abstract: Traditional in-memory graph storage systems have limited scalability due to the limited capacity and volatility of DRAM. Emerging persistent memory (PMEM), with large capacity and non-volatility, provides us an opportunity to realize the scalable and high-performance graph stores. However, directly moving existing DRAM-based graph storage systems to PMEM would cause serious PMEM access inefficiency issues, including high read and write amplification in PMEM and costly remote PMEM accesses across NUMA nodes, thus leading to the performance bottleneck. In this paper, we propose XPGraph, a PMEM-based graph storage system for managing large-scale evolving graphs, by developing an XPLine-friendly graph access model with vertex-centric graph buffering, hierarchical vertex buffer managing, and NUMA-friendly graph accessing. Experimental results show that XPGraph achieves 3.01× to 3.95× higher update performance and up to 4.46× higher query performance, compared with the state-of-the-art in-memory graph storage system implemented on a PMEM-based system.
papers.abstract_zh: 由于 DRAM 的容量和易失性有限，传统内存图存储系统的可扩展性有限。新兴持久内存（PMEM）具有大容量和非易失性，为我们提供了实现可扩展和高性能图存储的机会。然而，直接将现有的基于 DRAM 的图存储系统迁移到 PMEM 会导致严重的 PMEM 访问低效率问题，包括 PMEM 中的高读写放大以及跨 NUMA 节点的远程 PMEM 访问成本高昂，从而导致性能瓶颈。在本文中，我们提出了 XPGraph，一种基于 PMEM 的图存储系统，用于管理大规模演化图，通过开发具有以顶点为中心的图缓冲、分层顶点缓冲区管理和 NUMA 友好的图访问的 XPLine 友好的图访问模型。实验结果表明，与基于 PMEM 的系统上实现的最先进的内存图存储系统相比，XPGraph 的更新性能提高了 3.01 倍至 3.95 倍，查询性能提高了 4.46 倍。
